linked_issues.title: feat(server): Add support for `SAVE`, `BGSAVE`, `BGREWRITEAOF` commands
linked_issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Add support for the following persistence/server management commands across all language bindings (Java, Python, Node.js, Go):

- [`SAVE`](https://valkey.io/commands/save/)
- [`BGSAVE`](https://valkey.io/commands/bgsave/)
- [`BGREWRITEAOF`](https://valkey.io/commands/bgrewriteaof/)

These commands are already defined in the Rust core layer but are not yet exposed in any of the language client APIs.

### Use Case

- `SAVE` performs a synchronous RDB snapshot of the dataset to disk. Useful for taking point-in-time backups in controlled environments.
- `BGSAVE` triggers an asynchronous RDB snapshot in the background without blocking the server. The optional `SCHEDULE` flag allows the save to be scheduled if an AOF rewrite is in progress.
- `BGREWRITEAOF` triggers an asynchronous rewrite of the Append-Only File in the background, compacting the AOF log without blocking the server.
- `LASTSAVE` returns the Unix timestamp of the last successful `SAVE` or `BGSAVE`. Useful for monitoring backup recency and health checks.

### Proposed Solution

- Implement solution for one language to verify behaviour and lock down interface.
- Then raise corresponding pull request(s) for other languages.

### Acceptance Criteria

- Public methods added on the relevant interfaces for each language binding.
- Cluster client variants accept a `Route` / `RouteOption` where the command is node-targeted (`SAVE`, `BGSAVE`, `BGREWRITEAOF`, `LASTSAVE`).
- Return types follow existing conventions per language binding.
- Unit + integration tests covering happy path, error paths, and routing variants.
- Runnable examples for each new public API.
- `make lint` and relevant test targets pass for all affected bindings.

### Other Information

- These commands are already present in `glide-core/src/request_type.rs` (see #5078).
- `BGSAVE SCHEDULE` causes the save to be delayed until no other background save or AOF rewrite is in progress; handle this as a boolean/optional flag rat

title: Core, Java, Python, Node, Go: Add client-wide circuit breaker
body:
### Summary

Add a client-wide circuit breaker that detects sustained error rates in the Rust core and rejects requests at the FFI boundary before threads park. Opt-in, disabled by default. All clients get config exposure; Java additionally performs a synchronous pre-check to prevent thread explosion under `managedBlock()`.

### Issue link

Closes #5996

### Features / Behaviour Changes

- New `ClientCircuitBreakerConfiguration` config and `CircuitBreakerException/CircuitBreakerError` class in all clients (Java, Python, Node, Go)
- When enabled: CB tracks error rate in a sliding window, trips when threshold exceeded, rejects requests instantly while open, recovers automatically via optimistic HalfOpen with consecutive success validation
- Java: synchronous `is_healthy()` check at JNI boundary (same location as inflight check)
- Error reporting and CB pre-check centralized in Rust core's `send_command` so all clients benefit automatically
- Logging: error type breakdown on trip, rate-limited rejection count while open, recovery duration

### Implementation

- `glide-core/src/client/circuit_breaker.rs` — State machine (Closed/Open/HalfOpen), sliding window with 10K cap, failure rate threshold, `try_write()` for non-blocking health check, input validation
- `glide-core/src/client/mod.rs` — CB wired into Client struct, result reporting after `send_command`, pre-check rejection, `is_healthy()` exposed
- `glide-core/src/errors.rs` — `CircuitBreakerOpen` error type classification
- `glide-core/src/protobuf/response.proto` — `CircuitBreakerOpen = 4` in `RequestErrorType`
- `glide-core/src/protobuf/connection_request.proto` — `ClientCircuitBreakerConfig` message
- `glide-core/README.md` — Developer documentation for CB
- `java/src/lib.rs` — Sync pre-check at both JNI entry points with parameterized error code
- `java/src/jni_client.rs` — `complete_error_sync` accepts error code parameter
- `java/client/.../CircuitBreakerException.java` — Dedicated exception type
- `java/client/.../ClientCircuitBreakerConfiguration.java` + `ConnectionManager.java` — Config + validation + protobuf serialization
- Python: `CircuitBreakerError`, config class with validation, dispatch in `get_request_error_class()`
- Node: `CircuitBreakerError`, config interface with `ConfigurationError` validation, dispatch in `getRequestErrorClass()`
- Go: `CircuitBreakerError` struct, dispatch via C enum, config with validation

### Testing

- 10 Rust unit tests (state machine, concurrency, window eviction, validation)
- Java integration test (config accepted, normal operations unaffected)
- E2E docker benchmark: consistent results across runs showing CB reduces slow errors by ~50% and converts them to fast rejections
- Manual smoke tests passing for all 4 clients (Java, Python, Node, Go)

### Follow-up Work

- Expose CB config to remaining clients (valkey-glide-csharp, valkey-glide-php, valkey-glide-ruby)
- Documentation in valkey-glide-docs (CB configuration guide, best practices)
- Enhanced watchdog diagnostics ([#6044](https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-glide/pull/6044)) — use timeout cause classification, inflight trends, and p99 data for smarter
tripping and recovery
- Per-shard CB granularity (future iteration based on data collected)

E2E tests over 12h (6h with and 6h without CB) showed significant reduction in slow (timeout) errors, keeping the core moving quickly:
```
cb-test-runner  | ========== CLIENT CIRCUIT BREAKER BENCHMARK ==========

linked_issues.title: Add client-wide circuit breaker
linked_issues.body:
### Describe the feature

Add a per-client circuit breaker that detects when the GLIDE core is unhealthy and rejects requests synchronously at the FFI boundary before threads park. This is the MVP for circuit breaker functionality in GLIDE, with per-shard granularity as a potential follow-up based on data collected.

The CB lives in the Rust core as a single state machine per client instance. Each language client checks it synchronously before submitting a command (same location as the inflight limit check in [#5952](https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-glide/pull/5952)).

`command() → [1. check inflight limit] → [2. check client CB] → [3. submit to Rust core via FFI]`

State machine: Closed → Open → HalfOpen → Closed

- **Closed:** Normal operation. Errors tracked in sliding window.
- **Open:** Core unhealthy. Requests rejected immediately at FFI boundary. Probes allowed after open_timeout.
- **HalfOpen:** Optimistic recovery. Allows all traffic through. Requires N consecutive successes before closing (grace period). Full traffic is allowed because blocking the entire client during recovery defeats the purpose.

What trips it:
- Aggregate error rate across all nodes exceeds `failure_rate_threshold` within `window_size`
- Only transport errors count (IoError, FatalSendError, FatalReceiveError, connection dropped)
- Timeouts: opt-in via `count_timeouts` (default false, to avoid false positives under Tokio starvation)
- Minimum error count (`min_errors`) must be reached before rate is evaluated

### Use Case

Under partial degradation or Tokio starvation, the client accumulates errors across nodes. Without a client-wide CB, `ForkJoinPool.managedBlock()` threads continue parking and the pool explodes. The per-client CB detects the sustained error pattern and rejects at the FFI boundary before threads park, preventing thread explosion and preserving application stability.

Also applies to standalone mode where per-node/per-shard CB would not apply.

### Proposed Solution

title: fix(test): fix flaky `PAUSE`/`UNPAUSE` test
body:
### Summary

Remove the concurrent `GET` from `PAUSE`/`UNPAUSE` integration tests across all language clients to fix a flaky test failure caused by a race condition between `GET` and `SET` commands dispatched concurrently.

### Issue link

This Pull Request is linked to a flaky CI failure in `TestGlideTestSuite/TestClientPauseAllThenUnpause`:

```text
=== NAME  TestGlideTestSuite/TestClientPauseAllThenUnpause
    client_pause_test.go:94:
        Error Trace: .../go/integTest/client_pause_test.go:94
        Error:       Not equal:
                     expected: "before"
                     actual  : "after"
        Test:        TestGlideTestSuite/TestClientPauseAllThenUnpause
```

### Features / Behaviour Changes

:white_circle: None — test-only change.

### Implementation

The `clientPauseAll_then_clientUnpause` tests launched `GET`, `SET`, and `CLIENT UNPAUSE` concurrently during a `CLIENT PAUSE ALL` window. After the pause expired, the test asserted that `GET` returned the pre-pause value `"before"`. However, since all three commands are dispatched from separate concurrent execution contexts (goroutines in Go, async tasks in Python), their arrival order at `glide-core` is non-deterministic.

Other clients (Java, Node) were updated simply for cross-client consistency.

The fix removes `GET` from the concurrent pause window entirely. The test now launches only `SET` and `CLIENT UNPAUSE` concurrently, then verifies:

1. Both commands are blocked during the pause.
2. Both commands complete once the pause expires or is lifted.
3. A sequential `GET` after the pause confirms the `SET` wrote successfully.

### Limitations

:white_circle: None.

### Testing

Ran the modified tests locally 5 times without failure for each client.

### Checklist

Before submitting the PR make sure the following are checked:

- [x] This Pull Request is related to one issue.
- [x] Commit message has a detailed description of what changed and why.
- [x] Tests are added or updated.
- [x] ~~CHANGELOG.md and documentation files are updated.~~ (Test-only fix, no docs needed.)
- [x] Linters have been run (`make *-lint` targets) and Prettier has been run (`make prettier-fix`).
- [x] Destination branch is correct - main or release

- [x] ~~Make sure to update the documentation in the [valkey-glide-docs](https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-glide-docs) repository if necessary~~ (No API changes.)

linked_issues.body:
### Test Name

TestGlideTestSuite/TestClientPauseAllThenUnpause

### Test Location

go/integTest/client_pause_test.go:94

### Failure Permlink

https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey-glide/actions/runs/26998298290/job/79672685399#logs

### Frequency

Observed in scheduled CI run on 2026-06-05. Failed in 10 out of 19 Go jobs.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Run the FMT Go workflow on schedule
2. Observe failure in Go test jobs for TestClientPauseAllThenUnpause

### System Information

GitHub Actions (ubuntu-latest, x64 and aarch64)

### Language and Version

Go 1.22 and 1.24

### Engine Version

All versions (redis 6.2, 7.0, valkey 7.2, 8.0, 8.1, 9.0)

### Logs

```
client_pause_test.go:94:
    Error Trace:    /home/runner/work/valkey-glide/valkey-glide/go/integTest/client_pause_test.go:94
                    /home/runner/work/valkey-glide/valkey-glide/go/integTest/glide_test_suite_test.go:518
    Error:          Not equal:
                    expected: "before"
                    actual  : "after"
```

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Glide Version

main

### Expected Behavior

Test should pass reliably. The CLIENT PAUSE command should block writes and the value should remain "before" during the pause period.

### Actual Behavior

The test asserts the value is "before" after a CLIENT PAUSE ALL, but instead gets "after", indicating the pause did not effectively block writes in time.

### Possible Fixes

The test may have a race condition where the write completes before the pause takes effect. Consider adding a longer delay between CLIENT PAUSE and the verification read, or using a retry mechanism to confirm the pause is active before writing.

title: feat(client): Add `CLIENT PAUSE` and `CLIENT UNPAUSE` commands
body:
### Summary

Adds support for `CLIENT PAUSE` and `CLIENT UNPAUSE` commands across all four language bindings (Java, Python async + sync, Node.js, Go).

### Issue link

Closes #6035.

### Features / Behaviour Changes

- **Core (Rust):** `CLIENT PAUSE` and `CLIENT UNPAUSE` are added to the `AllSucceeded` cluster response policy so a broadcast returns a single `OK`.
- **Java:**
  - New `ClientPauseMode` enum.
  - New methods on `ConnectionManagementCommands` (standalone) and `ConnectionManagementClusterCommands` (cluster, with `Route` overloads).
- **Python:**
  - New `ClientPauseMode` enum.
  - New `client_pause` and `client_unpause` methods for both `glide-async` and `glide-sync`.
- **Node.js:**
  - New `ClientPauseMode` enum in `Commands.ts`.
  - New `clientPause` and `clientUnpause` methods on `GlideClient` and `GlideClusterClient` with `RouteOption` overloads on the cluster client.
- **Go:**
  - New `ClientPauseMode` and `ClientPauseClusterOptions` types in `go/options/`.
  - New `ClientPause`, `ClientPauseWithMode`, and `ClientUnpause` on the standalone `Client`.
  - New `ClientPauseWithOptions` and `ClientUnpauseWithOptions` on `ClusterClient`.

### Implementation

- The public API matches the proposal in the issue and stays consistent across languages.
- The core change in `glide-core/redis-rs/redis/src/cluster_routing.rs`: `CLIENT PAUSE` and `CLIENT UNPAUSE` are already routed to all primaries; without this change the broadcast would surface as an array of per-node replies, which would break the documented `OK` return type that every language binding advertises. Folding them into the existing `AllSucceeded` arm collapses the broadcast into a single `OK` and matches the standalone behavior.

### Testing

- **Java:** unit tests in `GlideClientTest` and `GlideClusterClientTest`; integration tests in `glide.standalone.CommandTests` and `glide.cluster.CommandTests`.
- **Python:** async tests in `python/tests/async_tests/test_async_client.py`, sync tests in `python/tests/sync_tests/test_sync_client.py`.
- **Node.js:** wire-format tests in `GlideClientInternals.test.ts`; integration tests in `GlideClient.test.ts` and `GlideClusterClient.test.ts`.
- **Go:** example tests in `go/connection_management_commands_test.go` and `go/connection_management_cluster_commands_test.go`.

### Checklist

Before submitting the PR make sure the following are checked:

- [x] This Pull Request is related to one issue.
- [x] Commit message has a detailed description of what changed and why.
- [x] Tests are added or updated.
- [x] CHANGELOG.md and documentation files are updated.
- [x] Linters have been run (`make *-lint` targets) and Prettier has been run (`make prettier-fix`).
- [x] Destination branch is correct - main or release

This is a proposal to deal with issues like https://github.com/GantMan/jail-monkey/pull/102

In our case, we'd like to keep using this lib but keep some granularity on root beer methods, to be able to ignore those that seem to trigger most false positive cases.

Not entirely sure this is the best solution, but it should be retro-compatible (keeping the default `isJailBroken` value) and continues to expose constant values. The caveat is that it still runs all the checks, even the ones we don't use. title: 🐛 fix: support passkey-enabled accounts and fix auth state lifecycle
body:
## Problem
Accounts protected by a passkey (login via **Log in by QR code**) could not complete login. The logs looped with `Failed to get telegram account: Request aborted`, and a stale verticle could additionally trigger `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY: UNIQUE constraint failed: telegram_record.id`.

Fixes #117

## Root cause
The authorization state machine did not mark several `AuthorizationStateWait*` / `LoggingOut` / `Closing` / `Closed` states as unauthorized. As a result a half-initialized or closed verticle kept being reused, and re-inserting its record hit the primary-key constraint.

## Changes
- Mark `Wait*`, `LoggingOut`, `Closing`, `Closed` states as `authorized = false`, and handle `AuthorizationStateWaitPremiumPurchase`.
- On `AuthorizationStateReady`, recover from a duplicate-key insert by cleaning up the stale verticle and updating the existing record instead of failing.
- `handleTelegramCreate` no longer reuses a closed/closing verticle.
- Web: handle the new wait states, surface a hint to use **Log in by QR code** when a passkey / 2FA password is required, and refresh the account list on authorization updates.

## Testing
Login now completes on a passkey-enabled account; the `Request aborted` loop and the unique-constraint error no longer occur.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: PilaScat

title: [ISSUE #15315] Optimize Skill upload precheck and conflict handling
body:
Please do not create a Pull Request without creating an issue first.

## What is the purpose of the change

Optimize Skill ZIP upload by adding a lightweight precheck flow for Skill name/version conflicts before the full package upload, making draft/reviewing conflict handling explicit, and keeping multi-skill ZIP parsing consistent when nested skill-like folders or invalid/non-skill folders are present.

## Brief changelog

- Add Skill upload precheck request/result models and wire the admin/console/maintainer-client upload precheck APIs.
- Update Skill upload service logic so `overwrite=true` handles single and batch draft overwrite consistently, blocks reviewing versions, and uses a valid uploaded version when it should create a newer draft.
- Improve Skill ZIP parsing for nested Skill directories, wrapper directories, macOS metadata files, invalid skill-like folders, and non-skill folders in multi-skill packages.
- Update the console upload dialog to parse Skill metadata locally, auto-run precheck after file selection, present draft overwrite/skip choices, and distinguish invalid/non-skill folders from blocked Skill conflicts.
- Add and update unit tests, console handler/proxy tests, OpenAPI scenario coverage, and Skill specs for the new upload behavior.

## Verifying this change

title: [ISSUE #15420] fix(ai): support lenient skill upload versions
body:
Please do not create a Pull Request without creating an issue first.

## What is the purpose of the change

This change improves Skill ZIP import behavior so short or non-standard uploaded versions from `SKILL.md`, `_meta.json`, or `targetVersion` do not block first-time or legacy uploads. The server now normalizes numeric short versions where possible, resolves a legal target draft consistently for precheck, single upload, and batch upload, and the next console shows when an uploaded version was converted.

linked_issues.title: Improve Skill ZIP upload handling for invalid versions
linked_issues.body:
## Problem

When importing a Skill ZIP, the version in SKILL.md, _meta.json, or the upload targetVersion can come from older tools or other ecosystems and may not match Nacos supported formats like x.y.z or vN.

Rejecting the upload directly is a poor experience, especially for first-time uploads and older consoles that still call the upload API without using precheck.

## Expected behavior

Skill ZIP upload should handle invalid uploaded versions consistently across precheck, single upload, and batch upload:

- For a new Skill, generate draft version 0.0.1.
- For an existing Skill without a draft, generate the next draft version from the current maximum version, for example online 1.0.0 plus uploaded version 1.0 creates draft 1.0.1.
- For an existing Skill with an editing draft, use the draft version and only overwrite it when overwrite=true.
- Precheck should keep the raw uploaded version in the response so the console can display that it was converted to the valid target version.

## Additional context

The next console should make this visible to users, for example: uploaded version 0.1 has been converted to valid version 0.0.1.

linked_issues.title: AI resource latest label should be server-managed
linked_issues.body:
## Issue Description

AI resource version labels currently allow the reserved `latest` label to be changed by normal label update flows. This can make `latest` point to an arbitrary version, remain pointed to an offline version, or be removed by a user label update.

This affects Skill, Prompt, and AgentSpec resources.

## Expected Behavior

- `latest` should be managed by the server.
- Publishing or force-publishing a version should keep the server-managed latest behavior, while the historical `updateLatestLabel` parameter is retained only for compatibility and marked deprecated.
- Online/offline transitions should recalculate `latest` from online versions and remove it only when no online version remains.
- Label update APIs should tolerate client-provided `latest` for compatibility, but ignore the client value and merge the current server-managed `latest` back into the effective labels.
- Console UI should show `latest` but prevent selecting, deselecting, creating, or deleting it.

## Module

AI Registry / Console

title: [ISSUE #15183] Add built-in AI importer presets
body:
Please do not create a Pull Request without creating an issue first.

## What is the purpose of the change

Add default AI resource importer configuration examples, a built-in skills.sh import source, and a safer console import selection experience.

This lets users quickly enable the official MCP registry source, Skill well-known source, and skills.sh source from `application.properties` without copying long configuration keys from documentation. The new skills.sh importer follows the skills.sh CLI discovery flow: search candidate summaries via `/api/search`, fetch the selected Skill snapshot via `/api/download/{owner}/{repo}/{skillId}`, package it as a standard Skill ZIP artifact, and preserve the concrete skills.sh source URL in `sourceMetadata.artifactUrl`.

For skills.sh search, blank queries default to `skill` so the initial console load does not hit the upstream minimum query length error. One-character queries are rejected locally with a clear validation error because skills.sh requires at least two query characters.

The console import dialog no longer selects search results by default. Users can explicitly select all current candidates, clear the selection, and still deselect individual candidates after selecting all. The `Import all valid` action now imports the accumulated valid set from candidates that the user selected and validated across multiple validation batches in the same source.

## Brief changelog

- Add commented `nacos.plugin.ai.importer.mcp.official.enabled` example.
- Add commented `nacos.plugin.ai.importer.skills.well-known.enabled` and `url` examples.
- Add commented `nacos.plugin.ai.importer.skills.skills-sh.enabled` example.
- Add a built-in `skills-sh` importer preset and SPI builder in `nacos-default-ai-importer-plugin`.
- Add skills.sh query boundary handling: blank query defaults to `skill`, one-character query is rejected locally.
- Update console MCP/Skill import selection so candidates are not selected by default, and add select-all/clear controls.
- Accumulate validated import candidates across validation batches for `Import all valid`.
- Regenerate `console-ui-next` static assets.
- Add unit tests for the skills.sh importer and default source provider.
- Update the AI resource import plugin spec in English and Chinese.

linked_issues.title: [Feature] Support importing AI resources from operator-configured external registries
linked_issues.body:
## Background

Nacos AI Registry already supports MCP Server and Skill management, but importing AI resources from external platforms is still fragmented.

Current MCP import supports importing from MCP registry-style data, but some flows allow users to provide import URLs or MCP endpoints directly. This may introduce SSRF or internal network probing risks. Skill import also needs a pluggable design because there is no single standard registry protocol across different Skill marketplaces.

## Proposal

Introduce a unified `ai-resource-import` plugin mechanism for importing AI resources such as MCP Servers and Skills.

The high-level design is:

1. Import plugins are responsible only for reading external sources and converting external data models into Nacos import artifacts.
2. Import execution should target Nacos resource operators/services, not backend storage directly.
   - For MCP, the operator can use the current `McpServerOperationService`.
   - After MCP is migrated to `ai_resource`, only the MCP operator implementation should change.
   - Import plugins should not care whether the backend uses `ai_resource` or legacy Config-backed storage.
3. Import sources should be configured by operators/administrators.
   - Users select a configured `sourceId`.
   - Users should not submit arbitrary import URLs, IPs, or auth tokens.
4. A unified import API should be introduced for Admin/Console usage.
5. Existing MCP import APIs should be kept as compatibility endpoints and internally routed to the new import flow.

## Suggested API

```
GET  /v3/admin/ai/import/sources
POST /v3/admin/ai/import/preview
POST /v3/admin/ai/import/execute

GET  /v3/console/ai/import/sources
POST /v3/console/ai/import/preview
POST /v3/console/ai/import/execute

```

Existing MCP APIs can be deprecated gradually:

```
POST /v3/console/ai/mcp/import/validate
POST /v3/console/ai/mcp/import/execute
GET  /v3/console/ai/mcp/importToolsFromMcp
```

They should be routed through a compatibility adapt

title: [ISSUE #15145] fix(ai): restrict forcePublish to valid states and add reedit with pipeline stale flag
body:
Please do not create a Pull Request without creating an issue first.

## What is the purpose of the change

Optimize `forcePublish` and `reedit` capabilities for AI resources (Skill, Prompt, AgentSpec). `forcePublish` is now restricted to `reviewed`/`reviewing` states with backward compatibility for legacy rejected-draft data. The `reedit` operation marks the pipeline as stale to prevent unintended forcePublish on re-edited drafts. Additionally, `deleteDraft` logic is unified across all three resource types, and the frontend is adapted to reflect these state restrictions.

## Brief changelog

- Restrict `doForcePublish` to only allow `reviewed`/`reviewing` states; legacy rejected-draft (stale != true) remains allowed for backward compat
- Add `PublishPipelineInfo.stale` field to distinguish reedit-draft from legacy rejected-draft
- In `doReEdit`, mark pipeline info as stale when transitioning reviewed → draft
- Unify `deleteDraft` logic into `AiResourceManager.doDeleteDraft` with `VersionStorageDeleter` callback pattern
- Frontend: check pipeline stale flag to conditionally hide forcePublish button; add delete button for reviewed versions
- Adapt legacy console-ui to display reviewed/rejected pipeline status correctly
- Default enable `ReviewedStatusConfig`
- Add unit tests for forcePublish allowed on legacy rejected-draft and blocked on stale draft

## Verifying this change

- `mvn test -pl ai` — all AI module unit tests pass including new forcePublish restriction tests
- Manual verification: reedit → draft (stale) → forcePublish blocked
- Manual verification: legacy rejected-draft data (stale=null) → forcePublish still allowed
- Frontend verification: stale pipeline hides forcePublish button, delete button shows for reviewed versions

* [x] Make sure there is a Github issue filed for the change (usually before you start working on it). Trivial changes like typos do not require a Github issue. Your pull request should address just this issue, without pulling in other changes - one PR resolves one issue.
* [x] Format the pull request title like `[ISSUE #123] Fix UnknownException when host config not exist`. Each commit in the pull request should have a meaningful subject line and body.
* [x] Write a pull request description that is detailed enough to understand what the pull request does, how, and why.
* [x] Write necessary unit-test to verify your logic correction, more mock a little better when cross module dependency exist. If the new feature or significant change is committed, please remember to add integration-test in [test module](https://github.com/alibaba/nacos/tree/master/test).

author: saynoword

linked_issues.title: [Feature] Restrict forcePublish to valid states and add pipeline stale flag for reedit
linked_issues.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

After the `reviewed` status was introduced (#15036), `forcePublish` still allows publishing from any non-online state including `draft`. This is problematic because:

1. A user who re-edits a reviewed version (reedit → draft) could still forcePublish without re-triggering the pipeline, bypassing the review process.
2. There's no way to distinguish between a "legitimately rejected draft" (legacy data, should allow forcePublish) and a "re-edited draft" (should block forcePublish).
3. `deleteDraft` logic is duplicated across Skill, Prompt, and AgentSpec with minor differences.

### Describe the solution you'd like

1. **ForcePublish state restriction**: Only allow `forcePublish` on `reviewed` or `reviewing` status. For backward compatibility with legacy data, also allow on `draft` when pipeline is REJECTED and not marked as stale.

2. **Pipeline stale flag**: Add a `Boolean stale` field to `PublishPipelineInfo`:
   - `null` (legacy data): forcePublish allowed on draft
   - `true` (after reedit): forcePublish blocked on draft

3. **Reedit marks pipeline stale**: When `doReEdit` transitions a reviewed version back to draft, set `stale = true` on the pipeline info.

4. **Unified deleteDraft**: Extract common deleteDraft logic into `AiResourceManager.doDeleteDraft` with a `VersionStorageDeleter` callback for resource-type-specific storage cleanup.

5. **Frontend adaptation**: Hide forcePublish button when pipeline is stale; show delete button for reviewed versions.

### Module

- [x] ai
- [x] console-ui-next
- [x] console-ui (legacy)

title: [ISSUE #15142] feat(ai): support drag-and-drop zip upload and batch skill upload
body:
Please do not create a Pull Request without creating an issue first.

## What is the purpose of the change

Add drag-and-drop zip upload support to the Skill Management page and introduce a batch upload endpoint that accepts multi-skill zip archives, enabling users to upload multiple skills at once with clear per-skill feedback.

## Brief changelog

- Add `BatchUploadResult` model in `api` module for cross-module accessibility
- Add `parseMultipleSkillsFromZip` to `SkillZipParser` returning `MultiSkillParseResult` that carries both parsed skills and parse failures
- Invalid skill folders (malformed YAML, missing description, etc.) no longer block the entire batch — they are recorded as failures and other skills continue uploading
- Detect sibling folders missing `SKILL.md` and report them as warnings in the batch result (showing only the folder name for clarity)
- Silently ignore well-known non-skill directories (`.git`, `__MACOSX`, `node_modules`, etc.)
- Extract `doUploadSingleSkill` private method to eliminate duplicate logic between `uploadSkillFromZip` and `batchUploadSkillsFromZip`
- Add `/v3/console/ai/skills/upload/batch` endpoint wired through console handler chain and maintainer-client
- Add HTML5 drag-and-drop with `dropEffect='copy'` and visual overlay to both `console-ui` and `console-ui-next`
- Add `batchUpload` API method in `console-ui-next` calling the batch endpoint
- Show per-skill colored results in toast (green checkmarks for success, red crosses with failure reasons)
- Add i18n strings for drag hints and batch upload results (en-US / zh-CN)
- Add unit tests for multi-skill zip parsing including invalid folder handling and nested structure detection

## Commits

- `ea4fc91c0` feat(console): support drag-and-drop zip upload in skill management
- `ea791f1ba` feat(ai): support batch upload skills from multi-skill zip archive
- `53751fcf8` refactor(console): simplify batch upload toast to show per-skill results
- `e418e636c` fix(ai): align resolveUploadVersion call with updated method signature
- `881cea0ce` feat(console): update frontend static assets for skill management UI
- `5f40e10e6` refactor(ai): improve batch upload resilience and deduplicate upload logic

## Verifying this change

- `mvn -B clean package apache-rat:check spotbugs:check -DskipTests`
- `mvn test -pl ai -Dtest=SkillZipParserTest` (23 tests, all pass)
- `mvn test -pl ai` (1200 tests, all pass)
- Manual: drag a multi-skill zip onto the Skill Management page, verify per-skill results display correctly
- Manual: drag a zip containing an invalid skill folder, verify the failure is reported with folder name

Follow this checklist to help us incorporate your contribution quickly and easily:

* [ ] Make sure there is a Github issue filed for the change (usually before you start working on it). Trivial changes like typos do not require a Github issue. Your pull request should address just this issue, without pulling in other changes - one PR resolves one issue.
* [ ] Format the pull request title like `[ISSUE #123] Fix UnknownException when host config not exist`. Each commit in the pull request should have a meaningful subject line and body.
* [ ] Write a pull request description that is detailed enough to understand what the pull request does, how, and why.
* [ ] Write necessary unit-test to verify your logic correction, more mock a little better when cross module dependency exist. If the new feature or significant change is committed, please remember to add integration-test in [test module](https://github.com/alibaba/nacos/tree/master/test).

linked_issues.title: [Feature] Support drag-and-drop zip upload with multiple skills
linked_issues.body:
What is the feature you'd like to see?
Support uploading AI skills by dragging a zip file into the Nacos console, with the following capabilities:
1. Drag-and-drop zip upload: Allow users to drag a .zip file directly onto the Nacos console UI to upload AI skill resources.
2. Multi-skill zip support: A single zip package should be able to contain multiple skill definitions, which Nacos will parse and register individually.
Why do you need this feature?
Currently, uploading skills one by one is tedious and error-prone for users who manage multiple skills. Supporting batch upload via a zip package would:
● Improve user experience with drag-and-drop interaction
● Enable batch registration of multiple skills in one operation
● Simplify migration and sharing of skill sets between environments
Suggested Solution
● Add drag-and-drop upload support in the console UI (console-ui module)
● Add a server-side API to accept zip file uploads, extract contents, validate skill definitions, and register them into the AI registry
● Define a standard zip structure, e.g.:
skills.zip
├── skill-a/
│   └── SKILL.md
├── skill-b/
│   └── SKILL.md
└── ...
Environment
● Nacos version: 3.x (develop branch)
● Relevant modules: console-ui, ai, ai-registry-adaptor

title: [ISSUE #14848] Add download Prompt version as Markdown feature
body:
## What is the purpose of the change

Fix #14848

Currently the Nacos console only supports viewing the content of a Prompt version inline, but there is no way for users to export or persist a Prompt version locally. This PR adds a feature that allows users to download any version of a Prompt as a Markdown file from the console, and tracks the download counts of each prompt / prompt version so that users can observe the popularity of prompts. The implementation is fully aligned with the existing Skill download feature for consistency.

## Brief changelog

**Backend**

- Add `PromptMarkdownBuilder` utility to render a `PromptVersionInfo` (including metadata, variables, template, and examples) into a well-formatted Markdown document.
- Add a new Console API `GET /v3/console/ai/prompt/version/markdown` in `ConsolePromptController`, returning the markdown content with `Content-Disposition: attachment` so that browsers trigger a file download.
- Add the full handler chain for the new API: `PromptHandler` / `PromptInnerHandler` / `PromptRemoteHandler` / `PromptNoopHandler` / `PromptProxy`.
- Add `downloadPromptVersion` in `PromptOperationService` / `PromptOperationServiceImpl`, which fetches the version detail and publishes a `PromptDownloadEvent`.
- Add `PromptDownloadEvent` (new event) and `PromptDownloadCountManager` (new `SmartSubscriber`) to accumulate download counts in memory and flush them to DB every 10 seconds using atomic SQL (`download_count = download_count + N`), keeping the counting logic isolated from the existing `SkillDownloadCountManager`.
- Add `downloadCount` field to `PromptMetaSummary` and `PromptVersionSummary`, and populate it in `listPrompts` / `listPromptVersions` / `getPromptDetail`.

**Frontend (console-ui-next)**

- Add `downloadPromptVersionMarkdown` API call in `src/api/prompt.ts`.
- In the Prompt detail page, add a download button using the existing `CliCommandCard` component on the right sidebar (aligned with Skill detail page).
- Display the total download count and per-version download count as `InfoCell` entries inside the Basic Info card.
- Update TypeScript types in `types/prompt.ts` (`downloadCount: number | null`).
- Add i18n keys `prompt.downloadCount`, `prompt.downloads`, `prompt.versionDownloads` in both `en-US.json` and `zh-CN.json`.

## Verifying this change

**Unit tests**

- Add `PromptMarkdownBuilderTest` covering header/metadata rendering, variables section, template section, examples section, and null/empty edge cases.

**Manual tests (with MySQL datasource, standalone mode)**

1. Open the Nacos console and enter the Prompt detail page.
2. Click the download icon inside the CLI Command card on the right sidebar — a `.md` file named `{promptKey}-{version}.md` is downloaded.
3. Open the downloaded file and verify the Markdown content contains the prompt metadata, variables, template, and examples.
4. Refresh the page (wait > 10s to let the counter flush to DB). Verify that the **Downloads** and **Version Downloads** values in the Basic Info card increase correctly.
5. Download different versions of the same prompt, and confirm that only the corresponding version's counter increments while the total count increments as expected.

**Checks**

- `mvn -B clean package apache-rat:check spotbugs:check -DskipTests` — passed
- `mvn clean install -DskipITs` — passed

linked_issues.title: nacos.extension.ai.enabled should fully disable AI registry console features
linked_issues.body:
<!-- Here is for bug reports and feature requests ONLY!

If you're looking for help, please check our mail list、WeChat group and the Gitter room.

Please try to use English to describe your issue, or at least provide a snippet of English translation.
我们鼓励使用英文，如果不能直接使用，可以使用翻译软件，您仍旧可以保留中文原文。
-->

**Describe the bug**
When `nacos.extension.ai.enabled` is set to `false`, AI registry related console features are not fully disabled. The backend configuration coverage is incomplete, related management APIs are still not fully guarded, and the console UI still shows AI related menus.

中文说明：当 `nacos.extension.ai.enabled` 设置为 `false` 时，AI Registry 相关能力没有被完整关闭：后端配置覆盖不全，管理接口控制不完善，同时前端相关菜单仍然存在。

![95yEwS](https://minio.pigx.vip/oss/202605/95yEwS.png)

**Expected behavior**
When `nacos.extension.ai.enabled=false`, AI registry related backend endpoints and console UI entries should be disabled consistently. Users should not see or access AI registry console features when the extension is turned off.

**Actually behavior**
Even after setting `nacos.extension.ai.enabled=false`, the console still displays AI related menus, and some backend management behavior is not fully controlled by this switch.

**How to Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Start Nacos with `nacos.extension.ai.enabled=false`.
2. Open the Nacos console.
3. Check the AI registry related menus and management functions.
4. The related console menu still exists and the switch does not fully cover backend management behavior.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: Any
 - Version: Nacos 3.x develop branch
 - Module: console / AI registry
 - SDK: original

**Additional context**
This issue is created to track the problem fixed by PR #14989.

Related PR: https://github.com/alibaba/nacos/pull/14989

title: feat: add microservice/ai function mode, support disabling AI registry in console, and add Makefile for rapid development
body:
https://github.com/alibaba/nacos/issues/15031

## What is the purpose of the change

Supports using `microservice` mode to enable both Service Registry and Configuration Center without loading AI-related modules, and adds a dedicated `ai` mode for AI-focused scenarios.

## Brief changelog

- Add `FUNCTION_MODE_MICROSERVICE` and `FUNCTION_MODE_AI` constants in `EnvUtil` to support the new modes
- Update `ConfigEnabledFilter` / `NamingEnabledFilter` to enable both Config and Naming under `microservice` and `ai` modes
- Update `AiEnabledFilter` so the AI module only loads when the mode is empty (default) or `ai`, avoiding unnecessary AI loading in `config` / `naming` / `microservice` modes
- Update `IstioEnabledFilter` / `K8sSyncEnabledFilter` to also activate in `microservice` mode
- Update `ConditionFunctionEnabled` so both `microservice` and `ai` activate the `@Conditional` beans for Config and Naming; change `ConditionAiEnabled`'s target from empty to `ai`
- Propagate `microservice` and `ai` values into the `nacos.function.mode` system property from `NacosCoreStartUp` and `NacosConsoleStartUp`
- Update console front-ends:
    - `console-ui-next` (new UI): sidebar filters AI Registry / Config / Service / Plugin menus by `functionMode`; new `DefaultRedirect` component redirects to `/skill`, `/serviceManagement`, or `/configurationManagement` based on mode
    - `console-ui` (legacy UI): `menu.js` adds `microservice` and `ai` branches; `Welcome.js` redirects to `skillManagement` in `ai` mode
- Update `distribution/bin/startup.sh` and `distribution/bin/startup.cmd` to recognize `-f microservice` / `-f ai` and forward them to `-Dnacos.functionMode`
- Add / update unit tests (`AiEnabledFilterTest`, `ConditionFunctionEnabledTest`, `NacosConsoleStartUpTest`, `NacosCoreStartUpTest`) to cover the new modes
- Add a `Makefile` with build and run targets for each function mode (`install-and-run-bootstrap`, `-config`, `-naming`, `-microservice`, `-ai`) to speed up local development and verification

`microservice` mode enables Service Registry and Configuration Center simultaneously without loading the AI module, which is a good fit for production deployments that only need the core Nacos capabilities. `ai` mode keeps Naming and Config loaded as backend dependencies while the console UI focuses on the AI registry pages.

## Verifying this change

| Function Mode                       | Service Registry | Configuration Center |   AI Features    |
|-------------------------------------|:----------------:|:--------------------:|:----------------:|
| `-Dnacos.functionMode=all` or empty |        ✅         |          ✅           |        ✅         |
| `-Dnacos.functionMode=config`       |        ❌         |          ✅           |        ❌         |
| `-Dnacos.functionMode=naming`       |        ✅         |          ❌           |        ❌         |
| `-Dnacos.functionMode=microservice` |        ✅         |          ✅           |        ❌         |
| `-Dnacos.functionMode=ai`           |   ✅ (backend)    |      ✅ (backend)     | ✅ (UI & backend) |

- `-Dnacos.functionMode=all` or empty: default mode. Original logic. Supports `Service Registry`, `Configuration Center`, and `AI features`
- `-Dnacos.functionMode=config`: Original logic. Supports only `Configuration Center`
- `-Dnacos.functionMode=naming`: Original logic. Supports only `Service Registry`
- `-Dnacos.functionMode=microservice`: New logic. Supports only `Service Registry` and `Configuration Center` (AI module not loaded)
- `-Dnacos.functionMode=ai`: New logic. Keeps Naming and Config loaded as backend dependencies; the console UI focuses on the AI Registry pages

Local verification via the newly added `Makefile`:

```bash
make install-and-run-bootstrap                # default mode (all)
make install-and-run-bootstrap-config         # config only
make install-and-run-bootstrap-naming         # naming only
make install-and-run-bootstrap-microservice   # naming + config (no AI)
make install-and-run-bootstrap-ai             # AI-focused mode
```

Follow this checklist to help us incorporate your contribution quickly and easily:

* [x] Make sure there is a Github issue filed for the change (usually before you start working on it). Trivial changes like typos do not require a Github issue. Your pull request should address just this issue, without pulling in other changes - one PR resolves one issue.
* [x] Format the pull request title like `[ISSUE #123] Fix UnknownException when host config not exist`. Each commit in the pull request should have a meaningful subject line and body.
* [x] Write a pull request description that is detailed enough to understand what the pull request does, how, and why.
* [x] Write necessary unit-test to verify your logic correction, more mock a little better when cross module dependency exist. If the new feature or significant change is committed, please remember to add integration-test in [test module](https://github.com/alibaba/nacos/tree/master/test).
* [x] Run `mvn -B clean package apache-rat:check spotbugs:check -DskipTests` to make sure basic checks pass. Run `mvn clean install -DskipITs` to make sure unit-test pass. Run `mvn clean test-compile failsafe:integration-test`  to make sure integration-test pass.
author: xuxiaowei-com-cn

title: feat: Embed AgentSpan agents into OSS Conductor (runtime + UI), gated by conductor.integrations.ai.enabled
body:
## Summary

Embeds the [AgentSpan](https://github.com/agentspan-ai/agentspan) agent framework into OSS Conductor as a first-class, **opt-in** capability, plus the agent management UI — all gated behind the existing `conductor.integrations.ai.enabled` flag. When the flag is off, Conductor behaves exactly as before (verified).

This lets OSS Conductor compile and run AI agents as **native Conductor workflows** (agent definition → `WorkflowDef` → `LLM_CHAT_COMPLETE`/tool tasks), with a UI to browse agents, executions, skills, and secrets.

Built on the published `org.conductoross:conductor-agentspan:0.2.0` library (Conductor deps are `compileOnly`, so this build supplies the engine). Downstream distributions that build on `conductor-ai` / `ui-next` (e.g. Orkes Conductor) inherit it.

## Backend (`feat(ai)`)

- `ai` module: `ConductorAgentSpanConfiguration` (gated by `AIIntegrationEnabledCondition`) component-scans `dev.agentspan.runtime`, re-declares `JOIN` as a primary bean to override core, and supplies the host SPI beans the library requires:
  - `EnvBackedCredentialStore` — read-only, environment-seeded credential store (`set()` → HTTP 400 directing operators to use env vars).
  - No-op `SecretOutputMasker`, worker-token signing key, request-principal filter, and skill metadata/package SPI adapters.
- `core`: `SkillMetadataDAO` + `SkillPackageDAO` interfaces.
- Persistence: Postgres / MySQL / SQLite / Redis implementations + Flyway migrations (`skill_metadata`, `skill_package`) so skills persist through the configured backend (package bytes Base64-encoded for uniform cross-backend binding). DAO beans gated on the flag.
- `server`/`server-lite`: `spring.main.allow-bean-definition-overriding=true` so AgentSpan's `HTTP`/`HUMAN`/`JOIN` task overrides take effect (harmless when AI is disabled).

## UI (`feat(ui)`)

- New agent pages rebuilt **natively on `ui-next` components** (DataTable, SectionHeader/Container, `useFetch`): **Agents** (`/api/agent/list`), **Executions** (`/api/agent/executions`, rows link into the native Conductor workflow viewer), **Skills** (`/api/skills`), **Secrets** (`/api/secrets/v2`, read-only).
- Gating: new `FEATURES.AGENTSPAN_ENABLED` drives the routes and an "Agents" sidebar submenu; default off in static `context.js`. `AgentSpanUiContextController` serves `/context.js` at runtime injecting `window.conductor.AGENTSPAN_ENABLED = <conductor.integrations.ai.enabled>` (mirrors orkes-conductor's `UIContextGenerator`).

## Testing / verification

- Backend validated end-to-end on **SQLite, Postgres, and Redis**: agent/skills/secrets REST APIs, byte-identical skill-package round-trips, and the AI-disabled regression (stock Conductor boots, `/api/agent` 404s).
- Ran the **AgentSpan Python SDK examples** (basic, tools, structured output, parallel, handoffs) against the embedded server with real OpenAI calls — all complete as native Conductor workflows.
- UI: `pnpm typecheck` + `pnpm build` green; full `ui-next` suite (590 tests) passing, including new route-gating + sidebar tests. Verified the server serves `/context.js` with the flag true/false, serves the agent SPA routes, and the pages render populated data after deploying/running agents.
- `spotlessApply`/`spotlessCheck` clean on touched Java modules.

## Notes

- MySQL skill DAO is implemented and structurally identical to Postgres, but a live MySQL boot wasn't completed due to a pre-existing scheduler-vs-main Flyway provisioning conflict (unrelated to this change).
- `conductor.integrations.ai.enabled=true` currently expects `conductor.db.type` ∈ {postgres, mysql, sqlite, redis*/memory}; Cassandra has no skill DAO impl yet.

title: fix(executions): restrict draft revision execution to the playground
body:
### What changes are being made and why?

Draft revisions must only ever run as **PLAYGROUND-kind executions**. This PR enforces that rule in the backend and fixes the two UI run entry points, which had the draft behaviour inverted.

#### Backend enforcement (`ExecutionController`)

- `createExecution` rejects an explicit draft revision unless `kind=PLAYGROUND` (422, checked before draft validation).
- The two replay endpoints reject an explicit draft revision when the source execution is not a playground one, so a draft cannot be smuggled into a NORMAL execution through a replay. Playground replays keep working since the replayed execution inherits the parent's kind.
- EE is covered too: its `ExecutionController` delegates to `super.createExecution`, and EE test suites emit the `Create` command directly (never through this endpoint), so TEST-kind runs are unaffected.

#### UI

- **Flow page (Execute dialog)** passed the loaded flow's revision to the create-execution API, so when the latest revision was a draft it executed the draft. It now omits the `revision` parameter for drafts, letting the backend resolve the **latest published revision** (and return the existing "save a published revision first" error for draft-only flows). Non-draft flows behave exactly as before.
- **Playground** passed no revision, so after a draft save the backend resolved the latest published revision and silently ran **stale code** instead of what is in the editor (or failed outright for draft-only flows). It now passes the current revision explicitly (with `kind=PLAYGROUND`), matching the playground's replay path which already did.
- **"Save & Execute"** in the editor now also omits the revision for drafts (running the latest published one, like the flow page) instead of sending a draft revision with `kind=NORMAL`, which the backend now refuses.
- The `draft_flow_warning` alert in the Execute dialog claimed the execution would run the draft, which is no longer true — reworded it in all 13 languages to say the latest published revision runs instead, and to point users at publishing or the playground to run their draft.

### Checks

- New tests in `ExecutionControllerRunnerTest`: draft + no kind → 422 (playground-only message); draft + `kind=PLAYGROUND` → execution created on the draft revision with PLAYGROUND kind. Existing invalid-draft validation test updated to pass the new gate. All 4 draft tests pass.
- `node check.js` (translations): no missing/extra keys
- `npm run check:types`: passes
- `FlowRunActions.spec.ts`: 2/2 pass

Fixes kestra-io/kestra-ee#8680
author: AcevedoR

title: perf: lazy-load plugin icons and sanitize SVG content (-12MB of JSON)
body: ### ✨ Description This PR refactors plugin icon handling to support lazy-loading individual icons on-demand instead of requiring the entire plugin-icons catalog to be preloaded. It also adds SVG sanitization to strip executable content from plugin icons before they're inlined into the DOM. **Key changes:** 1. **KsTaskIcon component refactor**: Converted from rendering SVG as a background image to rendering as an `` element with a `data:image/svg+xml;base64` URI. Added support for lazy-loading icons via a new `loadIcon` prop that resolves individual icons on-demand. 2. **SVG sanitization**: Added `SvgSanitizer` utility to strip script tags, event handlers, iframes, and javascript: URIs from SVG content before encoding. Plugin icons are now sanitized when loaded via `RegisteredPlugin.icon()`. 3. **Plugin icon API**: Added new lightweight endpoint `GET /plugins/icons/{cls}` to fetch a single icon instead of requiring clients to download the entire catalog. Extracted icon index computation into a cacheable method. 4. **Plugins store**: Added `loadIcon(cls)` method that caches requests and returns icons from the preloaded catalog if available, falling back to the new API endpoint for lazy-loading. 5. **Component updates**: Updated all components using `KsTaskIcon` to use the new `loadIcon` prop instead of passing the full `icons` map. Removed the duplicate `TaskIcon` component from the topology package. 6. **Editor integration**: Updated `KsEditor` to use `loadTaskIcon` prop for lazy-loading task icons in autocomplete suggestions. ### 🔗 Related Issue This change enables more efficient icon loading for views that only render a handful of task icons (execution timelines, trigger lists, etc.) without requiring the entire plugin catalog to be preloaded. ### 🎨 Frontend Checklist - [x] Code builds without errors - [x] All unit tests pass (KsTaskIcon tests updated to verify img element, data URI src, and lazy-loading behavior) - [x] Storybook stories updated with new LazyLoaded example ### 🛠️ Backend Checklist - [x] Code compiles successfully - [x] New unit tests added for SvgSanitizer (9 test cases covering script tags, event handlers, iframes, javascript: URIs, and harmless SVG preservation) - [x] New integration tests added for single icon endpoint (iconByClass and iconByClassNotFound) ### 📝 Additional Notes The SVG sanitization is applied at the source (when icons are loaded from plugin JARs), ensuring all icon delivery paths are protected. The lazy-loading mechanism includes request deduplication to avoid multiple concurrent requests for the same icon class. https://claude.ai/code/session_01KZE1CvUWoW6YAfiw3jVrwi
author: elevatebart

title: feat(core): REUSABLE_INPUTS input type, validation, and editor seam
body:
Open-source primitives for reusable inputs (the Enterprise feature that stores and resolves the definitions lives in the companion kestra-ee PR). Kept in OSS because the input `type` is a core enum used as Jackson's discriminator, so the type must be registered here for any flow to parse `type: REUSABLE_INPUTS`.

- New `REUSABLE_INPUTS` input type referencing a definition by `ref` (+ optional `namespace`/`revision`). Marked `@EeOnly` so it is excluded from the OSS-served flow schema — the open-source editor neither proposes nor flags it, while the EE-served schema includes it.
- `ReusableInputsExpander` interface with an `expand()` inlining primitive (the reusable-inputs counterpart of `Input.expandToLeaves`). The OSS default errors ("requires Enterprise Edition"); the EE build replaces it with a store-backed implementation.
- `FlowInterface.resolvableInputs(expander)` / `inlinedInputs(expander)` centralise reusable-inputs inlining alongside FORM flattening, so resolution paths inline references before flattening to dotted leaves; `FlowForExecution.of(flow, expander)` makes the execution form render and submit the resolved inputs under the reference id.
- `FlowValidator` rejects `@EeOnly` inputs at validation time on open-source (the EE build overrides to allow them), so a `REUSABLE_INPUTS` flow can't be saved on OSS.
- Editor: a generic `registerEditionProviders()` seam on the YAML language configurator (swapped per-edition via the `override` alias) lets the EE build attach the reusable-inputs hover / go-to-definition without shipping any reusable-inputs UI code in open-source; plus reusable-inputs sibling-input autocompletion in the definition editor. The execution form also skips an un-inlined reference node defensively.
- Docs: AGENTS.md now requires i18n for all user-facing UI strings.

Companion EE PR: https://github.com/kestra-io/kestra-ee/pull/8926
author: brian-mulier-p

title: [backport releases/v1.3.x] feat(plugindefaults): allow referencing named pluginDefaults by ref
body:
Backport of [#16446](https://github.com/kestra-io/kestra/pull/16446) to `releases/v1.3.x`.

- A `pluginDefaults` entry can declare an optional `ref`; tasks, triggers and task runners opt in via `pluginDefaultsRef`. A named default applies only to plugins referencing it (never by type matching), suppresses non-forced type-matched defaults for that plugin, and collapses same-`ref` entries to a single shadowing winner. A forced type-matched default always takes over entirely (ref ignored, WARN logged). Unresolved or type-mismatched refs are a validation error on create/update and fail at runtime (executor fails the task run, scheduler fails the trigger evaluation).
- **Re-implemented for the 1.3 architecture** (not a cherry-pick): 1.3 has the single `PluginDefault` model — the develop `FlowPluginDefault`/`PluginDefaultSpec` split from [#15812](https://github.com/kestra-io/kestra/pull/15812) is not present — and the runtime trigger guard is adapted to `AbstractScheduler` (1.3 has no `TriggerScheduler`).
- **⚠️ Behaviour differs from develop, by design:** on 1.3 flow-level `forced` is honored, so a flow-level `forced: true` `ref` bundle overrides a task's own value; on develop a flow cannot force (per [#15812](https://github.com/kestra-io/kestra/pull/15812)), so the task value wins. The same flow YAML therefore resolves differently across the two lines. Admin enforcement is preserved on both: a namespace/global `forced` `ref` still beats a flow `forced` ref via admin-first precedence.
- **Deliberate test gap:** develop's `TriggerSchedulerTest` case can't be ported (1.3's scheduler test harness is unrelated to develop's `TriggerScheduler`), and a 1.3 equivalent needs heavyweight JDBC-scheduler integration. The guard is verified by inspection — an unresolved trigger ref throws into the existing evaluate-loop catch then `handleFailedEvaluatedTrigger`, emitting a FAILED execution and leaving the trigger unlocked. The executor guard is covered end-to-end (`pluginDefaultsRefNotFound` on the H2 runner).
- Companion EE backport on the same branch name.

author: dskarzh

title: AI models: structured output support for more providers; fix Vertex AI location routing
body:
## Summary

Upgrades LangChain4j to `1.15.1-TB1` and migrates GitHub Models to the official OpenAI integration, which unlocks JSON Schema (structured output) support for additional AI providers in the AI request rule node.

Fixes https://github.com/thingsboard/thingsboard/issues/15705: the previous Vertex AI Gemini integration ignored the configured `location` and routed all requests to the global endpoint. The new `langchain4j-google-genai` integration passes the location to the google-genai SDK, which resolves the regional endpoint from it.

## Changes

**AI providers / structured output**
- Migrate GitHub Models from the deprecated Azure AI Inference integration to the official OpenAI integration and enable JSON Schema structured output for it.
- Expose JSON Schema as a response format for Anthropic and Amazon Bedrock (previously text-only in the rule node UI).
- Remove the manual JSON escaping previously applied to GitHub Models requests — the new integration escapes message content itself, so the workaround was causing double-escaping.

**Dependencies**
- Bump `langchain4j` to `1.15.1-TB1`; swap `langchain4j-google-ai-gemini`/`langchain4j-vertex-ai-gemini` for `langchain4j-google-genai`, and `langchain4j-github-models` for `langchain4j-open-ai-official`.
- Drop the `opennlp-tools` version pin — the upgraded fork ships `opennlp-tools >= 2.5.9`, so the CVE workaround pin is no longer needed.

**UI**
- Offer response formats per provider in the AI request node config and refresh the available model lists.

title: feat(iot-hub): set rule chain as Default rule chain on profile during install
body:
## Pull Request description

Adds an optional **Set for profile** action to the IoT Hub rule chain install dialog so installing a rule chain can also set it as the profile's **Default rule chain** in one step. Removes the need for the manual five-bullet readme step that operators previously had to perform after every rule chain install. The Calculated Field install flow is untouched.

The confirm step gains a third action — `Set for profile` — alongside `Cancel` and `Install`. Clicking it opens an entity picker restricted to `DEVICE_PROFILE` and `ASSET_PROFILE`. If the chosen profile already has a non-null `defaultRuleChainId`, a confirm-overwrite step names the existing chain before replacing it.

**Backend:**

- `RuleChainInstalledItemDescriptor` gains a nullable `EntityId targetProfileId`. Persists in the existing JSONB descriptor column — no schema migration. `@JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true)` on the base descriptor interface keeps old installed-item rows readable.
- `DefaultIotHubService.installRuleChain()` now accepts `SecurityUser` + `JsonNode data`. A new `setAsDefaultRuleChain()` helper applies the chain to the chosen profile through the existing `tbDeviceProfileService.save(...)` / `tbAssetProfileService.save(...)` audit-logged paths, so the profile change shows up as a normal tenant audit-log entry.

**Frontend:**

- `iot-hub-install-dialog.component` grows a per-`ItemType` `selectEntityConfig` map and a new `'confirm-overwrite'` dialog state. The CF flow now also reads from this map (same config it always had, just data-driven).
- `tb-entity-select` gets a `min-width: 512px` rule above the `gt-sm` breakpoint so the picker renders at a consistent width regardless of prompt-text length. Pattern matches `recipient-notification-dialog.component.scss`.
- New i18n keys under `iot-hub.*`: `rule-chain-install-desc`, `rule-chain-install-as-default`, `select-profile-for-rule-chain`, `rule-chain-overwrite-title`, `rule-chain-overwrite-body`, `rule-chain-overwrite-replace`.

**Documentation:** the manual "Set as Default Rule Chain on the relevant device profiles" step is removed from the rule chain template readme in the companion marketplace PR thingsboard/iot-hub#43. No thingsboard-ui-help update needed — no new public REST API was introduced.

**Tests:** no unit or integration tests were added. The `application/.../service/iot_hub/` package has zero existing test coverage today, and the change here matches that posture — `setAsDefaultRuleChain` is a deterministic switch over two entity types that both delegate to the already-tested `Tb*ProfileService.save()` methods. The rest of the change is descriptor wiring (one new field) and DI (`AssetProfileService` + `TbAssetProfileService` injected via Lombok `@RequiredArgsConstructor`).

Companion PRs:
- IoT Hub readme rewrite: thingsboard/iot-hub#43
- CE 4.3 follow-up (alarm rule prompt fix on top of the merged 4.2 work): #15646
- PE 4.2 / PE 4.3 mirror PRs to follow.

title: Hardened remote JS executor script invocation
body: Jira: [PROD-8016](https://thingsboard-portal.atlassian.net/browse/PROD-8016) ## Summary - Hardens the tb-js-executor sandboxed `invokeScript` so script arguments are constructed inside the sandbox context instead of being passed in from the host realm. - Marks `use_sandbox=false` as dangerous-by-design (yaml comment, startup WARN log, code comment) — kept as a documented performance trade-off for trusted, non-public clusters. - Addresses JVN#16937365 - Scope: `use_sandbox=true` path; `use_sandbox=false` is unchanged (intentional). > **Milestone — first unit tests in `msa/js-executor` with zero new devDependencies.** > Tests use Node's built-in `node:test` + `node:assert` (no Mocha/Jest/etc.). The only existing devDep reused is `ts-node`. `yarn test` is wired into the Maven `test` phase via `frontend-maven-plugin`, so TeamCity's `mvn test` picks them up automatically. Tests live in `test/` and are excluded from the production `pkg` bundle via `tsconfig`. ## Test coverage Three layers, each catches a different class of regression: | Layer | Location | What it asserts | |---|---|---| | Node unit (×4) | `msa/js-executor/test/jsExecutor.test.ts` | (1) `use_sandbox=true` blocks `args.constructor.constructor("return process")()` — throws `process is not defined`. (2) `use_sandbox=true` benign string args pass through. (3) `use_sandbox=false` documented contract: host process IS reachable (locks the unsafe-by-design behavior so any silent change forces a deliberate doc/threat-model update). (4) `use_sandbox=false` benign passthrough. | | Java unit (Spring MockMvc) | `application/.../RuleChainControllerTest#testScriptForbiddenForCustomer` | Customer JWT against `POST /api/ruleChain/testScript` → **403** with `"You don't have permission to perform this operation!"`. Locks in the existing `@PreAuthorize('TENANT_ADMIN')` guard so the surface stays tenant-admin-only. | | Black-box (live docker-compose) | `msa/black-box-tests/.../JsExecutorSandboxIsolationTest#testRuleChainScriptCannotReachHostProcess` | End-to-end via tb-node → Kafka `js_eval.requests` → tb-js-executor (`use_sandbox=true`). Asserts the response carries `error` containing `process is not defined` and `output` never reports `reachedHost=true`. | ### TDD verification (red → green) The Node unit tests were validated by reverting only `api/jsExecutor.ts` to the pre-fix state and running `yarn test`, then restoring the fix and re-running. | State | Test 1 — sandbox isolation (regression guard) | Test 2 — benign args passthrough | |---|---|---| | Without fix (red) | ✖ FAIL — `Missing expected rejection`, the host `process` was reachable | ✔ pass | | With fix (green) | ✔ pass — `process is not defined` thrown as expected | ✔ pass | So Test 1 is a true regression guard for the JVN escape; Test 2 protects the legitimate string-args contract. ## Why `runInContext` instead of `runInNewContext` `vm.Script` has two related methods that pair with different setup: | Method | Sandbox argument expects | What it does | |---|---|---| | `script.runInNewContext(obj, opts)` | a **plain** object | Contextifies `obj` on the fly, then runs the script. One-shot — no chance to touch the context first. | | `script.runInContext(ctx, opts)` | an **already-contextified** object (returned by `vm.createContext`) | Runs in a context that was set up beforehand. | The fix needs to do work **inside the sandbox context BEFORE the user's script runs** — specifically, evaluate `'[]'` in the sandbox so the array passed as `args` belongs to the sandbox's realm rather than the host's. To evaluate anything in a context you need a handle on it, and the only way to get a handle is `vm.createContext(...)`. Once the object has been contextified, `runInNewContext` would error (it expects a non-contextified object); the matching method is `runInContext`. `createContext` + `runInContext` is the long form; `runInNewContext` is the convenience wrapper that does both at once. Same V8 isolate machinery, different ergonomics. ## Test plan - [x] `yarn test` — 4 tests pass (sandbox + non-sandbox, isolation + passthrough) - [x] TDD verification: Node tests fail on pre-fix code, pass on fixed code - [x] `tsc --noEmit` clean - [x] `mvn test -N` (msa/js-executor) — runs `yarn test` automatically; nothing new appears outside `target/` - [x] `mvn -Dtest=RuleChainControllerTest#testScriptForbiddenForCustomer test` — Spring MockMvc returns 403 for customer JWT - [x] `mvn test-compile` for `msa/black-box-tests` — black-box test compiles cleanly - [x] CI build green — TestBlackBoxMicroservices [build #108671](https://builds.thingsboard.io/buildConfiguration/ThingsBoard_TestBlackBoxMicroservices/108671): 45/45 pass, including the new `JsExecutorSandboxIsolationTest#testRuleChainScriptCannotReachHostProcess` (227 ms, validates the fix end-to-end against the live docker-compose stack — response carries `error: process is not defined`) ci wired as well
author: smatvienko-tb

title: Added config property to control null ordering in dashboards
body:
## Pull Request description

Previously, there were two issues:

1.) Backend: the PostgreSQL query handled rows with null values incorrectly, excepting null values as 0. Old query:

select * from (select entities.*, (coalesce(cast(alias5.bool_v as varchar), '') || coalesce(alias5.str_v, '') || coalesce(cast(alias5.long_v as varchar), '') || coalesce(cast(alias5.dbl_v as varchar), '') || coalesce(cast(alias5.json_v as varchar), '')) as alias5_value, alias5.ts as alias5_ts, coalesce(alias5.dbl_v, cast(alias5.long_v as double precision), **(case when alias5.bool_v then 1 else 0 end)**) alias5_value_so_num,coalesce(alias5.str_v, cast(alias5.json_v as varchar), '') alias5_value_so_varchar from (select e.id id, 'DEVICE' entity_type, cast(e.name as varchar) as alias2, cast(e.label as varchar) as alias3, cast(e.additional_info as varchar) as alias4 from device e where e.tenant_id=:permissions_tenant_id and (e.id in (:entity_filter_entity_ids))) entities left join ts_kv_latest alias5 ON alias5.entity_id=entities.id AND alias5.key = (select key_id from key_dictionary where key = :alias5_key_id)  ) result  order by alias5_value_so_num asc, alias5_value_so_varchar asc, result.id asc limit 10 offset 0

When both dbl_v and long_v are NULL (rows with no value), the **CASE WHEN alias5.bool_v THEN 1 ELSE 0 END** evaluates to 0 instead of NULL, because CASE WHEN NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END returns 0 in PostgreSQL.

2.) UI: incorrect sorting for empty values

3.) For SQL-backed entity data queries added a configurable nulls order strategy (default, nulls_first, nulls_last)

title: Fixed base path variable
body:
## Description

This pr solves the issues with the base uri path. In React Scripts everything depended from a so called base tag. This base tag was rewritten when serving the frontend leading to the JavaScript and CSS being loaded from the rewritten tag in the Java code.  This pr simply takes all resource links and points them to a new location that is set via the properties.


## What does this pr not solve

During my testing I realized that you still need a proxy that rewrites the paths and fetches them from the original location. Only the paths in the index.html are rewritten. So the resource location needs to be configured via proxy config but this seems to be also the case before.


Closes #3742

linked_issues.body:
We have upgraded to 3.1.0. We are using `ZIPKIN_UI_BASEPATH` to rewrite the base path for the UI, but it doesn't seem to work anymore. Tried running:

```
docker run -p 9411:9411 openzipkin/zipkin:3.1.0
```

and the UI shows nicely on http://localhost:9411 (redirects to `http://localhost:9411/zipkin/`). With 

```
docker run -e ZIPKIN_UI_BASEPATH=/admin/zipkin -p 9411:9411 openzipkin/zipkin:3.1.0
```

it still redirects to `http://localhost:9411/zipkin/` but shows blank page. If I try `localhost:9411/admin/zipkin/` I get `404 Not Found`.

Perhaps I am missing some configuration changes for the upgrade?

title: [Improvement-16754][DataX] Support DataX writer parameter batchSize
body: ## Was this PR generated or assisted by AI? YES — used Claude (Sonnet 4.6 / Opus 4.7) to help review the original design, simplify the implementation (removing per-database dynamic defaults that were the wrong abstraction), and resolve rebase conflicts against `dev`. All design decisions, the final approach, and verification were reviewed by me. ## Purpose of the pull request DataX writer currently does not expose the `batchSize` parameter, so users are stuck with the DataX default of `2048`. This is too small for some target databases — e.g. the ClickHouse plugin recommends `65536`. This PR makes `batchSize` configurable from the task UI. Closes #16754 ## Brief change log Backend (`dolphinscheduler-task-datax`): - Add `batchSize` field to `DataxParameters` (lombok `@Data` generates accessors) - In `DataxTask`, write `batchSize` into the writer JSON only when value > 0, so the DataX upstream default still applies when the user picks `0 (unlimited)` - Extend `DataxParametersTest` to cover the new field Frontend (`dolphinscheduler-ui`): - Add a `batchSize` select control on the DataX task form - Static option list: `0 / 1024 / 2048 / 4096 / 8192 / 16384 / 32768 / 65536 / 131072` - Default `2048` (matches DataX upstream); ClickHouse / Databend users can pick `65536` / `131072` from the same list - Add `zh_CN` and `en_US` i18n entries (`datax_writer_batch_size`) **Design note**: an earlier draft switched the option list and default based on target database type (see [issue discussion](https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/issues/16754#issuecomment-4319892995)). That was dropped after review — `batchSize` optima depend on row width, network, and target hardware, not just on the database, so hard-coding database-specific defaults in the UI is the wrong abstraction. A static list keeps the UI consistent with surrounding fields (`jobSpeedByte` / `jobSpeedRecord` / `memoryLimit`). ## Verify this pull request This change added tests and can be verified as follows: - Added `testBatchSize` in `DataxParametersTest` covering the new field (default `0`, `2048`, `65536`) - Updated the existing `toString` assertion in `DataxParametersTest` to include `batchSize=0` - Manually verified locally: - Created a DataX task targeting MySQL with `batchSize=2048` — generated JSON contains `"batchSize": 2048` under `writer.parameter` - Set `batchSize=0` — generated JSON omits the field, DataX falls back to its own default - Switched UI between values, persisted task, reloaded — value preserved ## Screenshots ## Pull Request Notice [Pull Request Notice](https://github.com/apache/dolphinscheduler/blob/dev/docs/docs/en/contribute/join/pull-request.md)
