title: tests: code execution
body:
## Summary

Adds end-to-end and integration test coverage for Anthropic's code execution server tool, validating all sub-tools (`code_execution`, `bash_code_execution`, `text_editor_code_execution`), all tool versions (`code_execution_20250825`, `code_execution_20260120`, `code_execution_20260521`), streaming and non-streaming paths, programmatic tool calling with `allowed_callers`, web search auto-injection, file upload via `container_upload`, container reuse, and generated file retrieval.

## Changes

- Added 8 new Postman test cases to `provider-harness.json` covering Python code execution, bash sub-tool, text editor (create/view/str_replace), streaming SSE well-formedness, two newer tool versions (`20260120`, `20260521`), and programmatic tool calling with a custom `get_stock_price` tool using `allowed_callers`.
- Added `TestAnthropicCodeExecution` class to `test_anthropic.py` with 13 test methods covering the full code execution surface area, including a streaming accumulator regression guard (the `IndexError`/`UnicodeDecodeError` class of bug), multi-key `text_editor` input round-trip validation, the `files-api-2025-04-14` beta header path for `container_upload`, container reuse across requests, and generated file ID surfacing.
- Introduced `CODE_EXEC_TOOL_NAMES` / `CODE_EXEC_RESULT_TYPES` constants and a `_scan()` helper to classify and validate code execution blocks regardless of whether the SDK surfaces beta blocks as objects or dicts.

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Chore/CI

## Affected areas

- [ ] Core (Go)
- [ ] Transports (HTTP)
- [x] Providers/Integrations
- [ ] Plugins
- [ ] UI (React)
- [ ] Docs

## How to test

```sh
# Run the Python integration tests for the new code execution suite
cd tests/integrations/python
pytest tests/test_anthropic.py::TestAnthropicCodeExecution -v

# Run a specific sub-test, e.g. streaming regression guard
pytest tests/test_anthropic.py::TestAnthropicCodeExecution::test_02_python_streaming -v
```

The Postman collection can be run via Newman against a live Bifrost instance:

```sh
newman run tests/e2e/api/collections/provider-harness.json \
  --env-var baseUrl=<bifrost_url> \
  --env-var anthropicKey=<api_key>
```

Requires a valid Anthropic API key with access to `claude-opus-4-7` and the code execution tool. Test 11 (`container_upload`) additionally requires Files API access; it will auto-skip if unavailable.

## Breaking changes

- [x] No

## Related issues

## Security considerations

Code execution tests run inside Anthropic's sandboxed execution environment. No user-controlled code escapes the sandbox. API keys are passed via environment variables and never hardcoded. The `files-api-2025-04-14` beta header is only attached to the dedicated `files_beta_client` fixture and not leaked to other test paths.

## Checklist

- [ ] I read `docs/contributing/README.md` and followed the guidelines
- [x] I added/updated tests where appropriate
- [ ] I updated documentation where needed
- [x] I verified builds succeed (Go and UI)
- [ ] I verified the CI pipeline passes locally if applicable

title: mtpublisher: Add skeleton that pushes dummy cosignatures
body:
Add boulder-mtpublisher, a daemon that polls the MTC metadata keyspace for the latest checkpoint lacking a mirror cosignature and write a dummy one against the configured log. Give the dummy cosignature the right shape (a 72-byte Ed25519 timestamped_signature per c2sp.org/tlog-cosignature: an 8-byte BE timestamp followed by a 64-byte signature) but leave the signature bytes zero.

Also make the `mtcmeta_44947_4_1_0_44` schema available in Vitess. Rename `sa/db/01-mtca.sql` to `01-mtcmeta_44947_4_1_0_44.sql`, which is uglier. However, the Vitess test-server builds each keyspace's schema by globbing `sa/db/*<keyspace>.sql`, so the filename has to end with the keyspace name or the schema is (silently) left empty.

Fixes https://github.com/letsencrypt/boulder/issues/8762

title: lakectl fs upload - Multipart upload - generate presigned URL per part
body:
Uploading large files (tried with 25GB) via lakectl fs upload fails.
Encountered 2 issues:
1. API partNumber was limited to 1000 (but for 25GB we have 5000 parts)
   Fix: Raise the partNumber limit in the API spec from 1,000 to 10,000 to match S3's actual maximum.
2. All presigned URLs for multipart upload parts are generated upfront with 15 minutes expiration. With 5MB parts, later parts expire before they're uploaded.
    Fix: for each part, check if the presigned URL is expired. if so - regenerate it.

Fix [#979](https://github.com/treeverse/product/issues/979) 
Fix #10295

title: feat(setup): collect comm prefs during setup and validate email
body: Closes #10269 ## Change Description ### Background The setup flow currently uses two API calls: 1. `POST /setup_lakefs` migrates the database, creates the initial admin user, and returns one-time credentials. 2. `POST /setup_comm_prefs` saves email and communication preferences. This becomes risky once email validation is added to `setup_comm_prefs`. If `setup_lakefs` succeeds but `setup_comm_prefs` rejects the email, the credentials have already been created but have not been shown yet. If the user refreshes or leaves the page, those one-time credentials can be lost. ### Approach This PR extends `POST /setup_lakefs` to accept communication preferences and validate the email during setup. The WebUI now sends username and comm prefs in the same request. This approach was chosen because it fixes the credential-loss failure mode with the smallest change: - email is validated before irreversible setup work - comm prefs are saved in the same request - the setup flow stays simple - no new endpoint is needed If validation fails, setup returns `400` and nothing irreversible has happened. Backward compatibility is preserved because the new fields are optional. Existing callers can continue sending only `username`. In that case, setup completes without comm prefs, `comm_prefs_missing` is set to `true`, and the WebUI continues the existing follow-up flow by calling `/setup_comm_prefs` after initialization. #### Email validation checks Email validation now applies to both `/setup_lakefs` and `/setup_comm_prefs`. Checks performed: - format validation using `ParseAddress()`, - known public suffix validation using `golang.org/x/net/publicsuffix` to reject made-up TLDs, - disposable domain detection, and - free-provider detection. > [!NOTE] > [`github.com/AfterShip/email-verifier`](https://github.com/AfterShip/email-verifier) is used for disposable and free-domain classification. We only use its built-in classification lists, so validation stays local and fast. > This library was chosen to avoid maintaining our own domain lists in this PR. If we have concerns about the library, we can replace these checks with manually maintained lists without changing the overall setup flow. > [!NOTE] > Email validation does not use regex, and addresses like `no@no` are accepted since `.no` is a valid TLD. See: [Stop Validating Email Addresses with Regex](https://davidcel.is/articles/stop-validating-email-addresses-with-regex/). More stricter validation can be added in a follow-up if needed. ### Alternatives Considered 1. **Client-side validation**: would still need a server-side fallback, would require shipping and maintaining disposable/free domain lists in the browser (increasing bundle size), and would not protect direct API callers. 2. **Separate `POST /setup_validate_email` endpoint**: clean separation but adds a new API surface. 3. **Call `/setup_comm_prefs` before `/setup` in the WebUI**: This would preserve the two-call flow, but it requires us to rely on `setup_comm_prefs` being safe to call before initialization, both now and as the setup flow evolves. I chose not to couple the solution to that assumption when we could instead validate and persist comm prefs as part of the setup request itself. 4. **Keep the current API flow and change the WebUI to hold or immediately show credentials**: this keeps the API unchanged, but pushes the problem into frontend state management. Users can still refresh or leave before saving the one-time credentials, so the credential-loss problem is reduced but not eliminated. It also makes the setup flow more complicated and brittle by introducing UI-specific handling for a backend ordering problem. ### Behavior changes (that are worth highlighting) > [!IMPORTANT] > If setup completes without an email, we now explicitly persist comm prefs as missing during `POST /setup_lakefs`. That allows a later `GET /setup_lakefs` call to return `comm_prefs_missing: true` for installs created through this new flow. > > Previously, `POST /setup_lakefs` did not record any comm prefs state. As a result, once setup completed, `GET /setup_lakefs` treated the missing metadata as if comm prefs were already handled and returned `comm_prefs_missing: false`. In practice, that meant the WebUI would not show the follow-up comm prefs step, even though the user had never provided an email. ### Testing Details - **Unit tests (`pkg/api/email_validator_test.go`):** for email validation - **Controller tests (`pkg/api/controller_test.go`):** - **E2E tests (`webui/test/e2e/`):** - Updated fixtures to use `@treeverse.io` instead of `@example.com`. ### Breaking Change? No API surface removed. Additive schema change to `Setup`; `/setup_comm_prefs` remains available. Backward compatible for existing callers. **Behavioral:** Both endpoints now reject invalid/disposable/free emails where they previously accepted them. ### Screenshots **Invalid/Disposable email**: **Free Email**: ### How to Review Commit by commit: - [2c2666a97](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/2c2666a97?w=1) main changes to review. - [f2f13d6c9](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/f2f13d6c9?w=1) `make gen clients` - [5eab800ce](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/5eab800ce?w=1) add backend coverage and table-driven tests for email validation. - [5954e5593](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/5954e5593?w=1) add webui tests for setup page.

title: feat(webUI): add additional fields to setup page
body: ### Description This PR adds additional fields to the setup page alongside the existing email field. In WebUI, all of these fields are optional as discussed on https://github.com/treeverse/product/issues/1087. In API, the new fields are also optional to maintain forward compatibility with the older clients. Related: https://github.com/treeverse/product/issues/1087. ### Screenshots webUI Setup Page ### Future Work (not part of this PR) - Add email validation. ### Test plan Backward and forward compatibility has been manually verified: - Built lakeFS from master, ran setup with all new fields via the API → master accepted the request (200 OK) but silently ignored the new fields - Restarted on this branch with the same DB → confirmed it reads the data correctly (new fields missing from KV, defaults to empty) - Built lakeFS from this branch, ran setup with email only (simulating an old client) → confirmed new fields stored as empty strings, no errors - Built lakeFS from this branch, ran setup with all fields → switched to master and confirmed it starts and reads existing fields without issues (ignoring unknown keys) → switched back to this branch and confirmed all fields are read back correctly, data intact after the round-trip. Additionally: - E2E: Existing validation tests - Go: TestController_SetupCommPrefs — "all fields omitted" and "all fields provided" both return 200 - Manual: setup form layout and validation behavior ### How to review Commit-by-commit: 1. [bd58a848e](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/bd58a848e?w=1) — Go backend: api/swagger.yml (new optional fields), handling in controller and metadata, stats/ struct extensions 2. [d4b96f9f2](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/d4b96f9f2?w=1) — WebUI form: new fields, validations, and wiring to send the new fields with the API call 3. [9fd7b604b](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/9fd7b604b?w=1) — WebUI E2E tests 4. [8518de83e](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/8518de83e?w=1) — Client regen (auto-generated, can skim or skip) 5. [a8db98db1](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/a8db98db1?w=1) — Go tests for SetupCommPrefs 6. [501f43355](https://github.com/treeverse/lakeFS/commit/501f43355?w=1) - Make firstName, lastName, and companyName optional in WebUI as discussed in https://github.com/treeverse/product/issues/1087.

title: [Misc] Add brixbench benchmark module
body:
## Pull Request Description
This PR imports the initial `brixbench` benchmark module into the `aibrix` repository.

Included in this import:
- benchmark runner, resolver, deployer, driver, and observability packages under `brixbench/`
- benchmark scenarios, deployment manifests, and benchmark configuration files
- walkthrough documentation for the benchmark module
- module/import path updates for the nested `brixbench` module inside `aibrix`

Additional cleanup included in this PR:
- removed local-only and AI workflow metadata from the imported tree
- updated the module path to `github.com/vllm-project/aibrix/brixbench`
- removed bundled versioned release YAMLs from the repository
- added on-demand release artifact download and local cache logic under `.tmp/releases/<version>/`
- added resolver tests for release artifact download and cache reuse

Notes:
- Version-based release artifact resolution now downloads from GitHub releases at runtime instead of relying on checked-in release YAML files.

## May 21 2026

Addressed the latest review feedback and pushed follow-up commits.

Key updates:
- Removed the hardcoded gateway fallback and now require either a detected endpoint or an explicit BENCHMARK_GATEWAY_ENDPOINT override.
- Cleaned up the resolver schema to require provider explicitly, reject deprecated deployer, and preserve provider: null as the canonical plain vLLM baseline path.
- Added focused regression tests for gateway endpoint resolution, explicit provider: null handling, deprecated deployer rejection, and missing-provider rejection.

Verification:
- go test -v ./internal/resolver -count=1
- go test -v ./benchmark -run 'Test(FallbackGatewayEndpoint(Missing|Override)|ResolveGatewayEndpoint(UsesDetectedEndpoint|UsesOverrideOnDetectionFailure|FailsWithoutDetectedEndpointOrOverride)|ConfiguredMetricExporter|ExportMetricsIfConfigured|FormatScenarioRunIDUsesUTC|NowInUTCUsesUTCLocation)$' -count=1


## May 27 2026

Pushed follow-up updates for the latest review comments.

Changes:
- Moved the benchmark walkthrough into the brixbench README entry point.
- Added documentation for benchmark workload configs, especially vllmArgs such as request-rate, concurrency, num-prompts, dataset-name, goodput, and routing-specific options.
- Removed the large generated custom AIBrix manifests from testdata and kept the benchmark flow on release-artifact/workspace Helm-based deployment paths.

Verification:
- Confirmed the removed custom manifests are not referenced by active brixbench scenarios.
- Re-ran YAML/scenario reference validation.
- Re-ran the relevant Go tests.

title: fix: resolve trtllm metrics showing undefined model_name and engine_type
body:
## Pull Request Description

Fix trtllm Prometheus metrics emitting `model_name="undefined"` and `engine_type="undefined"` when using absolute model paths (e.g., `/models/Qwen3-8B`) in `trtllm-serve` startup commands.

### Root Cause

The TRT-LLM binary emits `model_name="undefined"` in its Prometheus output when given an absolute filesystem path instead of a HuggingFace model identifier. The aibrix Go metrics consumer (`cache_metrics.go`) propagated this literal `"undefined"` value to both Prometheus emission and internal pod record storage without validation or fallback.

Additionally, the Python metrics sidecar (`engine_rules.py`) had no trtllm engine rules defined — calling `get_metric_standard_rules("trtllm")` would crash with `ValueError`.

### Fix

**1. Go `pkg/cache/cache_metrics.go`** — Add "undefined" model name fallback in two locations:
- `worker()` ModelMetrics emit loop (line ~291): When `model == "undefined"`, fall back to the pod's `model.aibrix.ai/name` label
- `updatePodMetricsFromTypedResult()` (line ~496): Same fallback for internal pod record storage

This ensures both Prometheus-exported metrics and internal routing/autoscaling data use the correct model name.

**2. Python `python/aibrix/aibrix/metrics/engine_rules.py`** — Add `TRTLLM_METRIC_STANDARD_RULES`:
- 6 `RenameStandardRule` entries mapping unprefixed trtllm metrics to `aibrix:` namespace
- 1 `PassthroughStandardRule` for `request_queue_time_seconds`
- Register `"trtllm"` in `get_metric_standard_rules()`

Before:
```
e2e_request_latency_seconds_bucket{engine_type="undefined",model_name="undefined"} 0.0
```

After:
```
e2e_request_latency_seconds_bucket{engine_type="trtllm",model_name="Qwen3-8B"} 0.0
```

### Risk Assessment

**Low risk — additive changes only:**
- No changes to vllm/sglang code paths. The `"undefined"` check only triggers for that specific sentinel value.
- Go-side pod label fallback is the same pattern already used elsewhere in the codebase.
- Python rules follow the identical dict + `RenameStandardRule` pattern as `VLLM_METRIC_STANDARD_RULES` and `SGLANG_METRIC_STANDARD_RULES`.

## Related Issues

Resolves: #2008
Also addresses: #2004
Part of: #2001

## Testing

- [x] 36/36 Python metrics tests pass (including 4 new trtllm-specific tests)
- [x] New tests cover: trtllm rules existence, case-insensitivity, cross-engine standardization alignment
- [x] Existing vllm/sglang tests unaffected
- [x] `test_get_metric_standard_rules_not_support` still correctly rejects `"TensorRT-LLM"` (dashed form ≠ `"trtllm"`)

cc @varungup90 @Jeffwan
author: ianliuy

### 🚀 Feature Description and Motivation

there're some issues on the metrics fetching, If we use below config and then fetch the metrics
```
curl localhost:8000/prometheus/metrics
```

```
    containers:
      - args:
        - |
          cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/config.yaml
          backend: pytorch
          kv_cache_config:
            free_gpu_memory_fraction: 0.85
          max_num_tokens: 8192

          trust_remote_code: true
          return_perf_metrics: true

body:

title: Remove VPN Functionality
body:
The VPN functionality is being removed from zrok due to dependency management issues.

There is a migration guide that describes this change in more detail and lists options for workarounds.

It's possible that we'll consider layering on a new zrok-based VPN solution as a separate release.
author: michaelquigley

title: fix(sync): 避免 sync_aof=false 时缓存增量流
body:
## 背景

`sync_reader.sync_aof=false` 表示只同步 RDB、跳过增量 AOF，并在全量同步完成后退出。

当前实现会在 RDB payload 接收完成后无条件启动 AOF receiver，导致后续 replication stream 仍被写入本地 AOF backlog，但这部分数据不会发送到目标端。

## 改动

- 仅在 `sync_aof=true` 时启动 AOF receiver 和 PSYNC ACK。
- `sync_aof=false` 时关闭源端 replication 连接，避免缓存无消费者的增量流。
- 新增端到端回归测试：先等 reader 进入 `syncing rdb`，再写入增量 key，确认不会生成 `.aof` backlog，也不会增加 `aof_received_bytes`。
- 保留测试用例传入的 `advanced` 配置，避免测试 helper 覆盖它。

## 验证思路

这个 PR 保留三个提交，用来分别确认测试和修复有效：

- [第一个 commit](https://github.com/tair-opensource/RedisShake/pull/1050/commits/05b442b887207a587748d1705b30715a5e3dd524) 只加入测试和临时 workflow；对应 [测试任务](https://github.com/DCjanus/RedisShake/actions/runs/25978915149/job/76364001801) 在旧实现上失败，断言到 `0.aof`。
- [第二个 commit](https://github.com/tair-opensource/RedisShake/pull/1050/commits/caba1f84614f88e450538e3a8a76761b999b57a9) 只加入修复，不修改测试；同一个 [测试任务](https://github.com/DCjanus/RedisShake/actions/runs/25978937559/job/76364053200) 通过。
- [第三个 commit](https://github.com/tair-opensource/RedisShake/pull/1050/commits/ab3f79ddd254fa8e896873eb9c512b86cd3c0f12) 删除临时 workflow，最终只保留正式测试和修复。

这些测试任务来自 fork 分支 push 自动触发的 workflow。
author: DCjanus

title: fix: use FUNCTION LOAD REPLACE when restoring function libraries
body:
## Summary

- Append `REPLACE` when emitting `FUNCTION LOAD` from RDB-parsed function library entries, so sync no longer aborts when the destination already holds the same library (e.g. after a redis-shake restart, retry, or against a previously-synced destination).
- Add black-box tests covering both the RDB reader and sync reader code paths. Tests pre-load a different library with the same name on dst, then verify dst ends up with src's version after sync.

## Why

Reported in #1044: Valkey-to-Valkey function sync fails with:

```
ERR [writer_127.0.0.1_7013] receive reply failed. cmd=[function load #!lua name=mylib ...], error=[ERR Library 'mylib' already exists]
```

In a sync context the source is authoritative and functions are code (not user data), so unconditionally replacing matches both Valkey/Redis's own internal replication semantics (`FUNCTION RESTORE FLUSH`) and user expectations.

## Test plan

- [x] `go build ./...`
- [x] New test `tests/cases/function_library.py` (`test_function_library_rdb_replace_existing`, `test_function_library_sync_replace_existing`) passes locally with the fix applied.
- [x] Verified the new RDB test fails when the fix is reverted (assertion: dst still returns `dst_version` instead of `src_version`).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: suxb201

title: fix: discard RDB data when sync_rdb=false instead of writing to disk
body:
## Summary

- When `sync_rdb=false`, `receiveRDB()` was still creating a `dump.rdb` file on disk. The RDB payload was received and written but never read back — wasting disk I/O / space, and the file was never cleaned up.
- Now, when `sync_rdb=false`, the RDB stream is written to `io.Discard` so the Redis replication protocol is satisfied without touching the filesystem.
- Added integration test `test_sync_rdb_false` that verifies: no `dump.rdb` on disk, RDB keys not synced to dst, AOF incremental data synced correctly.
- Refactored all test cases: `import pybbt` directly (no alias), split `@pybbt.case() def main()` into individual `@pybbt.case()` functions for better isolation.

Closes #1015

## Test plan

- [x] `test_sync_rdb_false`: validates no dump.rdb file exists, RDB data is discarded, AOF data syncs correctly
- [x] All existing integration tests pass (`python -m pybbt cases/` — 14/14 passed)
- [x] `go build ./...` compiles cleanly

🤖 Generated with [Qoder][https://qoder.com]

title: fix: use RESTORE command in RDB parser to respect rdb_restore_command_behavior
body:
## Summary

The `rdb_restore_command_behavior` config was not being respected in RDB mode because the RDB parser used individual commands (SET, HSET, RPUSH, etc.) which silently overwrite existing keys without triggering BUSYKEY error.

### Changes

- Use `io.TeeReader` to capture raw RDB value bytes (simpler approach, same as before the perf commit)
- Use RESTORE command instead of individual commands in parseRDBEntry
- Fall back to individual commands for large values exceeding `target_redis_proto_max_bulk_len` with warning log
- Add Python tests for `rdb_reader`/`sync_reader`/`scan_reader`
- Update documentation with clear applicability table

### Applicability

| Reader | Phase | Uses RESTORE | Config Applies |
|--------|-------|--------------|----------------|
| `rdb_reader` | RDB | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| `sync_reader` | RDB phase | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |
| `sync_reader` | AOF phase | ❌ No (forwards commands directly) | ❌ No |
| `scan_reader` | Full/KSN | ✅ Yes | ✅ Yes |

### Limitation

For large values exceeding `target_redis_proto_max_bulk_len`, individual commands are used instead of RESTORE, and this configuration may not be respected.

Fixes: #996

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

title: Fix zebra nexthop cache skip check for unreachable nexthops
body:
The "already up-to-date" check in `applyToPathList` never short-circuited for unreachable nexthops because it compared the path's MED against `math.MaxUint32`, but MED is never set to that value - only the `IsNexthopInvalid` flag is set.
This caused redundant `updatePath` calls on every watcher cycle, creating a feedback loop that races with legitimate NHT updates and makes the `zebra-nht` scenario test flaky.

Split the check into separate reachable/unreachable cases so each is evaluated with the correct fields.

title: feat(errortracking): wire error-tracking handler and add E2E tests
body:
> **Stacked PR 4/4** — part of [#50607](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-agent/pull/50607). Builds on [stack-3 #52487](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-agent/pull/52487).

### What does this PR do?

Wires the error-tracking handler into the agent and validates the full pipeline end-to-end:

- **Config defaults** — adds `buffer_size`, `flush_interval`, `bouncer_window` to `common_settings.go`
- **Log setup** — wires `errortracking.Handler` into `buildSlogLogger` as a synchronous sibling handler
- **Agent run command** — installs an `fx.Lifecycle` hook that starts/stops the handler
- **E2E test suite** (`test/new-e2e/tests/agent-telemetry/`) — provisions a real AWS EC2 VM via Pulumi, installs the agent with a Python check that logs errors and raises an exception. Asserts that error log payloads reach the internal telemetry intake. Tests both `errortracking.enabled: true` and the default disabled state.
- **CI** — extends `.on_agenttelemetry_or_e2e_changes` with `test/new-e2e/tests/agent-telemetry/**/*`

### Stack
1. [stack-1 #52485] Core `pkg/util/log/errortracking/` package
2. [stack-2 #52486] agenttelemetry component extensions + sender refactor
3. [stack-3 #52487] FakeIntake aggregator/client + CI scaffolding
4. **This PR** — agent wiring + E2E test
author: pducolin

title: [procmgr] Add Windows Agent Data Plane supervised by dd-procmgr
body:
### What does this PR do?

Adds Windows Agent Data Plane (ADP) packaging and supervision via dd-procmgr:

- MSI ships `agent-data-plane.exe` (and FIPS crypto DLLs when applicable).
- Fleet installer `postinst` writes `processes.d/datadog-agent-data-plane.yaml` (same pattern as DDOT on Windows).
- ADP runs under dd-procmgr only; there is no `datadog-agent-data-plane` Windows SCM service (unlike DDOT, which keeps `datadog-otel-agent` as fallback).
- `processes.d` is listed in `PathsToRemoveOnUninstall()` for uninstall/rollback cleanup.

Also enables Windows in ADP omnibus packaging (Saluki artifacts), pins 1.2.2 Windows artifact hashes in `release.json`, and allows `data_plane.enabled` on Windows via `sanitizeDataPlaneConfig`.

### Motivation

ADP is already packaged on Linux and macOS. Windows needs the same capability with procmgr as the sole supervision path.

### Describe how you validated your changes

- Updated `sanitizeDataPlaneConfig` unit tests for Windows support.
- Windows install e2e checks `processes.d/datadog-agent-data-plane.yaml` is created.
- CI: Windows MSI build, install/upgrade e2e tests.

**Planned manual validation:**
- [ ] VM install: confirm `processes.d/datadog-agent-data-plane.yaml` exists and ADP runs under dd-procmgr when `data_plane.enabled` is true
- [ ] Upgrade/rollback: confirm `processes.d` cleanup does not affect DDOT yaml on failed upgrades

### Additional Notes

title: tools: datasource management
body:
## Summary

This PR expands the existing datasources tool set to be able to:

- create and update datasources with schema guidance based on the datasource type and health check to make sure the updates are working as expected
    - if a call is made to create a datasource with an ambiguous type (i.e. azure), the tool relies on `search_plugin_information` to help fetch related datasources and ask user for clarification.
    - if a datasource creation is attempted against a plugin that is not yet installed, the tool will install it first, then proceed with creation.
- run health checks on single ( by UID ) or multiple (by type) or all datasources in a grafana instance


## Notes
- right now the datasource schemas are as static JSONs, we are in the process of getting these schemas to be exposed via the plugin, and once available, we will have a follow up PR to replace these static files with an API call.
- This PR comes from multiple previous PRs that were approved and merged into the feature branch:
    1. https://github.com/grafana/mcp-grafana/pull/784
    2. https://github.com/grafana/mcp-grafana/pull/812
    3. https://github.com/grafana/mcp-grafana/pull/886 

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds write paths that mutate Grafana datasource configuration at scale; mitigations include read-only gating, secret field exclusion from guidance, and a mandatory schema review step, but misconfiguration or partial field mapping could still break datasources.
> 
> **Overview**
> Expands MCP **datasource** tooling so agents can **create and update** Grafana datasources with **type-specific schema guidance**, optional **post-create health checks**, and **bulk health checks** (by UID, type, or all).
> 
> **Schema-backed two-step create/update:** For plugin types with embedded `*_schema.json` files, the first `create_datasource` / update call returns **LLM-oriented field guidance** (common fields like `uid` / `isDefault` plus safe `root` / `jsonData` fields). **Secrets, `secureJsonData`, and credential usernames are omitted** from guidance. A second call with **`schemaReviewed=true`** and collected **`fields`** actually provisions the datasource. Types without a schema still prompt for explicit params (at minimum **`name`**) before creation.
> 
> **Infrastructure wiring:** `AddDatasourceTools` now takes an **`enableWriteTools`** flag so create/update register only when writes are allowed (aligned with `--disable-write` / read-only server mode). Examples and `cmd/mcp-grafana` pass this through like other write-gated categories.
> 
> **Coverage and tests:** Adds a large set of static per-plugin schema JSONs plus Go helpers to **embed, load, and filter** them. New Python integration test exercises the **`create_datasource`** two-call flow end-to-end via an LLM tool loop.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit daa71c0f84ab03780c0fe7df3a76b7609b77d527. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: jcolladokuri

linked_issues.title: MCP: Schema and Validation Exposure
linked_issues.body:
Parent: #7

## Summary

Expose Stream 1 schema and validation APIs through MCP.

## Context

Agents need access to datasource schemas and deterministic validation before creating or updating datasources.

## Scope

- Schema retrieval via MCP
- Validation via MCP
- Dependency-aware validation via MCP
- Schema/version metadata passthrough

## Tasks

title: feat(navigation): add shorten_url tool for Grafana short links, close #820
body:
### Summary

- Add a new `shorten_url` navigation tool to create Grafana short links, close #820 
- Keep `generate_deeplink` unchanged and support the chained workflow: generate_deeplink -> shorten_url.
- Register shorten_url only when write tools are enabled.
- Update navigation docs and tool tables in README.md and docs.
- Add/extend Go + Python tests for short-link behavior and tool-chain usage.

### Test plan

- [x] `GOMODCACHE="$(go env GOPATH)/pkg/mod" go test ./tools -run "TestShortenURL|TestGenerateDeeplink" -count=1`

- [x] `GOMODCACHE="$(go env GOPATH)/pkg/mod" go test ./cmd/mcp-grafana -count=1`

- [x]  `python3 -m py_compile tests/navigation_test.py`


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Introduces authenticated POSTs to Grafana’s short-url API and write/read-only tool registration changes; failures are handled by returning long URLs instead of erroring.
> 
> **Overview**
> **`generate_deeplink`** gains an optional **`shorten=true`** flag that, after building a dashboard/panel/Explore URL, calls Grafana **`POST /api/short-urls`** and returns a **`/goto/<uid>`** link when possible. If shortening fails or the server runs with **write tools disabled** (`--disable-write`), callers still get the full deeplink (read-only mode accepts `shorten` but ignores it).
> 
> Navigation registration now follows the same write/read-only split as other tool categories: **`AddNavigationTools(mcp, enableWriteTools)`** is wired from **`main`**, and server capability text mentions optional shortening. Docs describe the **`shorten=true`** flow; Go unit tests cover the short-URL client (auth, public vs API base URL) and fallback behavior, plus a Python eval for Explore + shortened URLs.
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 193ed0b4abb915f66eb283651c0f397c49ac6a5e. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>

author: leoswing

linked_issues.title: Add tool to create Grafana short URLs (/goto/<uid>) via /api/short-urls
linked_issues.body:
### Problem

`generate_deeplink` produces fully-qualified Explore URLs that embed the entire panes/queries JSON in the `left=` query parameter. For non-trivial LogQL/PromQL queries these grow to several hundred characters of URL-encoded JSON, which is awkward to:

- include in chat messages, PR descriptions, or wiki pages without breaking layout
- copy-paste from terminals (line-wrap whitespace insertion can corrupt the URL)
- display in plain-text contexts where the link text is the URL itself

Grafana already exposes a server-side shortener at `POST /api/short-urls`:

```http
POST /api/short-urls
{"path": "/explore?left=%7B...%7D"}

→ {"uid":"abc123","url":"https://grafana.example.com/goto/abc123"}
```

It would be useful to surface this from the MCP server so a model can chain `generate_deeplink → shorten_url` (or have the shortening done as part of `generate_deeplink` via an opt-in flag).

### Proposed API

Either a new tool:

```
shorten_url(url: string) -> string
```

or an opt-in flag on the existing tool:

```
generate_deeplink(..., shorten: bool = false) -> string
```

Behavior: POST the path portion of the URL to `/api/short-urls`, return the resulting `/goto/<uid>` URL.

### Use case

Top-N production error reports posted to Slack, where each row carries a Loki Explore deeplink. The query strings are long enough that the markdown source is hard to read and review; resolving to short links would make the report source ~10× smaller.

### Notes

- The endpoint requires the same auth the MCP server already uses for queries; no new credential.
- Long URLs would still work — short URLs are an opt-in convenience.
- Tested on the user side: `mcp-grafana` v0.13.0 + Grafana 12.x — `generate_deeplink` works correctly post-PR #644, but produces long URLs.

title: feat(influxdb): add InfluxDB datasource support (Flux + InfluxQL)
body:
## Summary

Adds a `query_influxdb` tool as a thin shim over Grafana's `/api/ds/query` endpoint, supporting both InfluxQL (v1.x) and Flux (v2.x). Follows the ClickHouse pattern so Grafana handles protocol details and this server inherits upstream fixes.

Closes #176.

## What's included

- **New tool `query_influxdb`** — accepts `datasourceUid`, `query`, optional `dialect` (`influxql` / `flux`), time range, and `maxDataPoints`. Dialect is inferred from the datasource's configured version when omitted.
- **Dashboard integration** — `run_panel_query` now routes InfluxDB panels, picks the dialect from the panel's `queryType`, and reuses the existing template-variable and macro substitution.
- **Query examples** — `get_query_examples` returns 8 curated starter queries (4 Flux, 4 InfluxQL) so assistants can pick a dialect without round-tripping schema discovery.
- **Empty-result hints** — InfluxDB-specific suggestions when a query returns no points (check bucket/database, retention, tag filters, etc.).
- **Docs** — new guide `query-metrics-with-influxdb.md`, row added to the tools reference table, `--disable-influxdb` documented in the flags reference, feature entry in README.
- **Test infrastructure** — docker-compose `influxdb` + `influxdb-seed` services using InfluxDB 2.7 with ~6h of seeded cpu/mem points; two provisioned Grafana datasources (Flux + InfluxQL via v1-compat) sharing one backend.

## Scope

Intentionally narrow, matching the #176 spec:

- **In:** `query_influxdb` with Flux and InfluxQL, `run_panel_query` routing, `get_query_examples`, empty-result hints.
- **Out:** Dedicated metadata tools (`list_measurements`, `list_tag_keys`, etc.) — these diverge across InfluxDB v1 / v2 / v3 and would triple the surface area. Users can run `SHOW MEASUREMENTS` or Flux `schema.*` via `query_influxdb`.
- **Out:** InfluxDB v3 SQL dialect — thin adoption today. The dialect validator is structured so adding it later is a one-line change.

## Why `/api/ds/query` instead of a direct InfluxDB SDK

ClickHouse follows this same pattern ([tools/clickhouse.go](tools/clickhouse.go)) and it composes cleanly with the rest of the server:

- No new third-party dependency (`influxdb-client-go` not added).
- Grafana's InfluxDB datasource plugin owns the v1/v2 protocol details; when Grafana fixes bugs, this server inherits them.
- Works with any Grafana deployment where the datasource is already configured (managed Grafana, self-hosted, etc.).
- Reuses the existing auth/TLS/OrgID/UserAgent transport stack — no new auth surface.

## Test plan

All commands run locally against docker-compose.

- [x] `go build ./...` — ok
- [x] `go vet` (unit + integration tags) — ok
- [x] jsonschema comma linter — clean
- [x] `go test -tags unit ./...` — all packages pass (adds 15+ new unit test cases)
- [x] `go test -tags integration ./tools/ -run 'TestInfluxDBIntegration|TestRunPanelQuery_InfluxDB_E2E|TestDatasourcesTools'` — 13 tests pass against live Grafana 12.4.2 + InfluxDB 2.7:
  - 6 × `TestDatasourcesTools` (datasource count updated 10 → 12)
  - 4 × `TestInfluxDBIntegration_*` (Flux, InfluxQL, dialect override, wrong type)
  - 3 × `TestRunPanelQuery_InfluxDB_E2E/*` (Flux panel, InfluxQL panel, multi-panel)

## Notes for reviewers

- Provisioned two datasources (`influxdb-flux` + `influxdb-influxql`) against one InfluxDB backend so both dialects have test coverage without running two containers.
- Hardcoded admin token `mcptests-admin-token` in docker-compose and provisioning — intentional for local tests only, matches the existing pattern used by Elasticsearch and ClickHouse.
- Added `.gitattributes` to enforce LF line endings for `*.sh` files so shell-based seed scripts keep working when the repo is checked out on Windows.
- Datasource-count assertion in `TestDatasourcesTools/list_datasources` was already stale (said "10" with 9 enumerated); updated the comment to match the 12 now provisioned.

---

First PR to this repo, happy to split into smaller commits, drop scope, or restructure anything that doesn't match house style. Thanks for considering it!

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Adds a new datasource query path (`query_influxdb`) and routes `run_panel_query` through it, plus new docker-compose services and provisioning for integration tests; risk is mainly around correctness of `/api/ds/query` payload/response handling and test infrastructure changes.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds **InfluxDB datasource querying** via a new `query_influxdb` tool that forwards raw Flux/InfluxQL through Grafana’s `/api/ds/query`, including dialect inference/override, time range support, and row-flattening with empty-result hints.
> 
> Extends `run_panel_query` to execute InfluxDB panels (deriving dialect from panel `queryType` when present), and expands query examples and hinting to cover InfluxDB.
> 
> Updates docs/CLI flags and tool tables to include the new optional `influxdb` category, and augments CI/dev/test infrastructure with an InfluxDB container, seed script, provisioned Flux+InfluxQL datasources, and new unit/integration/E2E coverage (plus LF enforcement for shell scripts via `.gitattributes`).
> 
> <sup>Reviewed by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/bugbot) for commit 25cf733e4dcc97fa7353c399c1d06514a6e6fe59. Bugbot is set up for automated code reviews on this repo. Configure [here](https://www.cursor.com/dashboard/bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: ap-rose

linked_issues.title: Feature request: InfluxDB datasource
linked_issues.body:
It would be extremely helpful if **mcp-grafana** could query an **InfluxDB datasource**—whether it’s a self-hosted node on EC2 or an InfluxDB Cloud bucket. Right now we have to write extra glue code just to perform a simple aggregation like min, max, and mean temperature across multiple sensors over the last 30 days.

### Proposed approach
* **New tool:** `query_influxdb`
  * **Inputs**
    * `datasource_uid` – Grafana UID of the InfluxDB datasource  
    * `query` – raw query text  
    * `start`, `end` – RFC 3339 or epoch timestamps; omit both for an instant query  
    * `dialect` – one of `sql` (default), `flux`, or `influxql`
  * **Behaviour**
    1. Build a payload for Grafana’s `/api/ds/query`, mapping `query` into `rawSql`, `queryText`, or the InfluxQL field according to `dialect`.  
    2. Forward the request unchanged.  
    3. Return the response JSON exactly as received (same pattern as `query_prometheus` and `query_loki_logs`).

### Motivation
* Removes boilerplate for both self-hosted and cloud InfluxDB users.  
* Keeps the tool signature consistent with existing datasource helpers.  
* Supports all three InfluxDB query languages without breaking older setups.


Please let me know if this aligns with the roadmap. I'll be happy to contribute.

linked_issues.title: Alert rule tools emit bare boolean JSON Schema values, breaking Fireworks AI and other providers
linked_issues.body:
## Description

The `create_alert_rule` and `update_alert_rule` tools emit bare boolean JSON Schema values (e.g., `"model": true`) in their tool definitions. While valid per the JSON Schema specification (`true` means "accept any value"), LLM providers like **Fireworks AI** reject these with a 500 `internal_server_error`.

## Reproduction

1. Connect mcp-grafana to any LLM gateway that routes to Fireworks AI
2. Include `create_alert_rule` or `update_alert_rule` in the tool list
3. Send a chat completion request with 26+ tools (including the alert rule tools)
4. Fireworks returns: `{"error":{"object":"error","type":"internal_server_error","code":"invalid_request_error","message":"server had an error while processing your request, please retry again after a brief wait"}}`

With 25 tools (excluding alert rule tools): succeeds.
With just `create_alert_rule` alone: fails.

## Root Cause

The alert rule params include Go `interface{}` fields (likely `QueryData` or the alert rule model). When reflected to JSON Schema via the `jsonschema` library, `interface{}` produces a bare `true`. Fireworks AI's schema parser does not handle bare boolean schemas.

## Affected Tools

- `create_alert_rule` — has `"model": true` in schema
- `update_alert_rule` — same

## Expected Behavior

Bare boolean schemas should be replaced with equivalent object schemas:
- `true` → `{"type": "object"}` (or more specific if the type is known)
- `false` → `{"not": {}}`

## Workaround

We applied a `sanitizeSchema()` function in our MCP adapter layer that recursively converts bare booleans before passing schemas to the LLM provider. See: androidStern-personal/openclaw-mcp-adapter#9

## Environment

- mcp-grafana: Docker image `grafana/mcp-grafana` (latest as of 2026-02-23)
- Grafana: 11.x with Editor-role service account
- LLM Provider: Fireworks AI (Kimi K2.5 model)
- Gateway: LiteLLM v1.81.13 + OpenClaw v2026.2.22

linked_issues.title: create_alert_rule and update_alert_rule tools emit "model": true in JSON Schema, breaking Google ADK / Pydantic-based MCP clients
linked_issues.body:
The Bug
Affected repository: [grafana/mcp-grafana](https://github.com/grafana/mcp-grafana)

Issue tracker: https://github.com/grafana/mcp-grafana/issues/new

Where the error originates
File 1 — The tool definitions using []*models.AlertQuery:

https://github.com/grafana/mcp-grafana/blob/v0.11.2/tools/alerting.go

Lines 576 and 690 define the Data field for CreateAlertRuleParams and UpdateAlertRuleParams:

Data []*models.AlertQuery `json:"data" jsonschema:"required,description=Array of query data objects"`
File 2 — The upstream AlertQuery model with interface{}:

https://github.com/grafana/grafana-openapi-client-go/blob/main/models/alert_query.go

The Model field is defined as:

Model interface{} `json:"model,omitempty"`
What happens
When Go's jsonschema reflection generates a JSON Schema from AlertQuery, the interface{} type for Model gets serialized as the boolean true — which is technically valid JSON Schema (it means "any value allowed"):

```json
{
  "properties": {
    "data": {
      "items": {
        "properties": {
          "datasourceUid": { "type": "string" },
          "model": true,              // <-- THE BUG
          "queryType": { "type": "string" },
          "refId": { "type": "string" }
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```
What it should be
The model property should be a proper JSON Schema object instead of a bare boolean:
`"model": { "type": "object" }`
Or at minimum:
`"model": {}`

Both are valid JSON Schema for "any value" but expressed as objects that Pydantic can parse.

Why it breaks
Google ADK (used by kagent) converts MCP tool schemas to Gemini format using Pydantic's _ExtendedJSONSchema.model_validate(). Pydantic expects every property value to be a dict/object, not a boolean. When it hits "model": true, it fails with:

pydantic_core._pydantic_core.ValidationError: 1 validation error for _ExtendedJSONSchema
properties.data.items.properties.model
  Input should be a valid dictionary or object to extract fields from
  [type=model_attributes_

title: Backport: stop false stale-send guard (#2115 + prerequisite #2034) to release/v0.9.x
body:
## Summary

Backport of the chat send-guard fix to `release/v0.9.x`. The send guard falsely blocked the next message with "New messages loaded — please review before sending" on essentially every turn after the first — sending twice was needed each time.

This backport is **two cherry-picks**, in order:

- `#2034` (`e1422ab4`) — `fix(ui): avoid false stale chat send guard`. Prerequisite.
- `#2115` (`df828c0d`) — `Stop false stale-send guard after tool-call turns`. The actual fix.

## Why #2034 is included (not just #2115)

`release/v0.9.x` shipped an older, **count-based** send guard: it blocks when `extractMessagesFromTasks(tasks).length > localMessageCount`, comparing raw message counts. On that guard, the just-completed turn lives in `streamingMessages` and is not counted in `localMessageCount` (which counts only `storedMessages`) until a block triggers a reload — so every send after the first blocks once, reloads, and succeeds on retry. That is the "send twice every message" symptom.

`#2115` makes streamed and persisted messages key identically (`contextId`/`taskId`), which only matters for the **key-based** guard introduced by `#2034`. Applied alone to 0.9.x it would be a no-op (backfilling ids does not change a count). So `#2034` is cherry-picked first to bring the key-based guard, then `#2115` fixes it.

Note for reviewers: `#2034` is a behavioral change to the guard (count-based → key-based comparison of comparable messages), not only a bugfix. Both cherry-picks applied with no conflicts (0.9.x's `ChatInterface.tsx`/`messageHandlers.ts` were identical to `#2034`'s parent).

## What #2115 fixes (on top of #2034)

Persisted agent messages omit `contextId`/`taskId` (A2A optional fields; the task is the canonical carrier). The locally-streamed copies carry the task's real ids, so the key-based guard keys them as `["task", …]` while the backend-extracted copies fall back to `["message", <id>]` (regenerated for tool messages). They never match, the backend looks ahead of the local view, and the send blocks on every turn with an agent response.

The fix carries the ids on agent messages so the copies key identically:

- **UI** (`ui/src/lib/messageHandlers.ts`): when flattening `task.history`, backfill `contextId`/`taskId` from the task for every extracted agent message (text and tool), treating `""` as absent. Repairs already-persisted sessions regardless of producer.
- **Runtime** (source): stamp the ids at emission time in both producers — Python `kagent-adk` (`convert_event_to_a2a_message`) and the Go ADK executor (`go/adk/pkg/a2a/executor.go`).
author: iplay88keys

title: Stop false stale-send guard after tool-call turns
body: ## Description The chat send guard falsely blocked the next message with "New messages loaded — please review before sending" on essentially every turn after the first — sending twice was needed each time. It surfaced first on a tool-call turn, but the root cause affects every agent response. ## Root cause A2A marks per-message `contextId`/`taskId` as optional — the `Task` is the canonical carrier, so persisted history messages routinely store them as empty strings. After a turn completes the UI keeps the locally-streamed message copies (it does not reload from the backend), and those carry the task's real `contextId`/`taskId`, so the send guard keys them as `["task", contextId, taskId, contentSignature]`. The backend-extracted copies, with empty ids, fall back to `["message", ]` instead — and for converted tool messages that id is even regenerated (`uuidv4`) on every extraction. So a persisted agent message never matches its locally-streamed counterpart, the guard counts the backend as ahead of the local view, and blocks the send. Every turn has an agent response, so every send after the first tripped it. ## Fix Carry `contextId`/`taskId` on agent messages so the streamed and persisted copies key identically. Two layers: - **UI** (`ui/src/lib/messageHandlers.ts`): when flattening `task.history`, backfill `contextId`/`taskId` from the task for **every** extracted agent message (text and tool), treating `""` as absent (`||`, not `??`). This is the complete fix on its own — it repairs already-persisted sessions and works regardless of which runtime produced them. - **Runtime** (source, defense-in-depth, helps new sessions): stamp `contextId`/`taskId` onto agent messages at emission time, in both producers — Python `kagent-adk` (`convert_event_to_a2a_message`) and the Go ADK executor, now the default declarative runtime (`go/adk/pkg/a2a/executor.go`, via a `newAgentStatusEvent` seam). A2A allows omission, and remote agents are out of our control, so the UI backfill stays regardless. Scope is limited to agent messages; user messages are keyed by `messageId` and already match. ## Reproduction 1. Send a message that triggers a tool call. 2. Send another message → blocked by the stale-send banner with no actual change.
author: iplay88keys

title: feat: substrate support for BYO and python runtimes for SandboxAgent CR
body: - Implements substrate support for BYO typed agents and the python runtime for declarative agents. - Fixes an existing issue where on config changes the previous golden snapshot was still being served. We now correctly serve the newest golden snapshot with the updated config. - Fixes an issue where golden snapshot building could use stale config. Substrate caches config values based on the `KAGENT_CONFIG_JSON` secret key ref name which is static. This can result in stale config being fetched. The secret key ref name now contains the config hash as a suffix so different configs now have a unique cache key. **Testing** - Tested locally in a kind cluster. Below is an example conversation with a declarative sandbox agent using the python runtime where I switch between model providers to test config rollout.

title: fix(mcp): handle null tools field in MCP server configs
body:
When tools is omitted from an MCP server config, the Go side serializes it as null. This causes a pydantic ValidationError on the Python side because the field expects list[str].

Fix: add omitempty to the Go JSON tags so null is never sent, and add a field_validator in Python to coerce null to an empty list.

Fixes #1797
author: mesutoezdil

linked_issues.title: [BUG] Agent pod CrashLoops with pydantic ValidationError when RemoteMCPServer ref omits toolNames (http_tools[*].tools=null)
linked_issues.body:
### 📋 Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](./issues) to avoid creating a duplicate
- [x] By submitting this issue, you agree to follow our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
- [x] I am using the latest version of the software
- [x] I can consistently reproduce this issue

### 🎯 Affected Service(s)

Multiple services / System-wide issue

### 🚦 Impact/Severity

Blocker

### 🐛 Bug Description

When an `Agent` (`kagent.dev/v1alpha2`) lists a tool of `type: McpServer`
referencing a `RemoteMCPServer` **without** an explicit `toolNames:` filter,
the controller materializes the agent's `config.json` Secret with
`http_tools[*].tools = null` (and `sse_tools[*].tools = null` for the SSE
protocol). The Python ADK runtime image
(`cr.kagent.dev/kagent-dev/kagent/app:0.9.1`) then crashes on startup with a
pydantic `ValidationError`, leaving the agent pod in `CrashLoopBackOff`.

Whether `toolNames` is intended to be required or optional, the current
behavior is a bug because omitting it silently produces a config that the
runtime cannot load, with no controller-side validation or admission error.

The mismatch is between two sides of the same wire format:

* **Controller (Go)** — `HttpMcpServerConfig.Tools` is `[]string` with the
  JSON tag `json:"tools"` (no `omitempty`). When `mcpServerTool.ToolNames`
  is `nil` in
  [`go/core/internal/controller/translator/agent/adk_api_translator.go`](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/blob/main/go/core/internal/controller/translator/agent/adk_api_translator.go#L911-L937),
  `encoding/json` marshals the nil slice as JSON `null`.
* **Runtime (Python)** —
  [`HttpMcpServerConfig.tools`](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/blob/main/python/packages/kagent-adk/src/kagent/adk/types.py#L143-L154)
  is typed `list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)`, which rejects `None`
  with a `list_type` validator error.

The CRD schema for `McpServerTool.toolNames` does not appear to mark

title: RemoteMCPServer TLS + companion-Secret API + plaintext egress rewrite
body:
# Description

Adds TLS configuration to `RemoteMCPServer` so declarative agents can connect to MCP servers behind private CAs, self-signed certs, or corporate internal CAs. Mirrors the existing `ModelConfig.spec.tls` shape so the operator-facing surface is consistent across resource types.

```yaml
apiVersion: kagent.dev/v1alpha2
kind: RemoteMCPServer
metadata:
  name: corp-mcp
  namespace: kagent
spec:
  description: Corporate-CA MCP server
  url: https://mcp.corp.internal/mcp
  tls:
    caCertSecretRef: corp-ca
    caCertSecretKey: ca.crt
```

`https://` URLs that the agent's system trust already covers don't need `spec.tls` — omit the block and TLS origination uses Go's default trust store. The CRD only rejects the contradictory `http://` + `spec.tls` combo.

```yaml
spec:
  url: https://api.example.com/mcp   # public CA, no spec.tls block needed
```

The companion egress URL rewrite (details in the `core/pkg/egress` bullet below) is opt-in via a new helm value; off by default. Enable when MCP traffic should egress to a TLS-terminating proxy:

```yaml
controller:
  mcpEgressPlaintext: true
```

## Egress URL-rewrite contract

| # | `spec.url` | `spec.tls` | CRD | `EffectiveScheme` | `EffectivePort` | `RewriteDialURL` (egress on) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `host/path` (scheme-less) | nil | ✅ | `http` | 80 | `http://host:80/path` |
| 2 | `host:1234/path` | nil | ✅ | `http` | 1234 | `http://host:1234/path` |
| 3 | `host/path` | `{}` | ✅ | `https` | 443 | `http://host:443/path` |
| 4 | `host/path` | non-empty | ✅ | `https` | 443 | `http://host:443/path` |
| 5 | `host:1234/path` | `{}` or non-empty | ✅ | `https` | 1234 | `http://host:1234/path` |
| 6 | `http://host/path` | nil | ✅ | `http` | 80 | `http://host:80/path` |
| 7 | `http://host:8080/path` | nil | ✅ | `http` | 8080 | `http://host:8080/path` |
| 8 | `http://host/path` | `{}` or non-empty | 🛑 | — | — | (CRD rejects) |
| 9 | `https://host/path` | nil | ✅ | `https` | 443 | `http://host:443/path` |
| 10 | `https://host/path` | `{}` | ✅ | `https` | 443 | `http://host:443/path` |
| 11 | `https://host/path` | non-empty | ✅ | `https` | 443 | `http://host:443/path` |
| 12 | `https://host:8443/path` | `{}` or non-empty | ✅ | `https` | 8443 | `http://host:8443/path` |

Invariants the table pins:
- `spec.tls != nil` is the TLS opt-in signal (even an empty `{}` counts); an explicit `https://` URL is a parallel signal. Either one is sufficient.
- Only one combination is admission-rejected: `http://` URL + non-nil `spec.tls` (row 8). `https://` with no `spec.tls` (row 9) is admitted and defaults to system trust.
- `RewriteDialURL` resolves every `spec.url` to the same plaintext URL — `http://host:<effective-port>/<path>` — using the tls-aware effective port. The agent's tool URL (via `RewriteConfigForAgent`) and the controller's tool-discovery probe dial an identical endpoint.

Things to note:

- `RemoteMCPServer.spec.tls *TLSConfig` reuses the `TLSConfig` from `ModelConfig`. The `caCertSecretRef`/`caCertSecretKey`/`disableSystemCAs` CEL validation rules were lifted onto the `TLSConfig` type itself so every consumer (ModelConfig today, RemoteMCPServer in this PR, future resources) gets identical admission validation without re-declaring rules per spec.
- CRD admission on `RemoteMCPServerSpec`: `spec.tls` must be unset when `spec.url` carries the `http://` scheme (a TLS opinion contradicts a plaintext URL). `https://` with no `spec.tls` is admitted and defaults to system trust.
- The controller reads `RemoteMCPServer.spec.tls` so that listing tools with a custom CA cert works. The tool-discovery dial uses the same TLS config the agent will use at runtime.
- `RemoteMCPServer.status.secretHash` captures the bytes of the referenced CA Secret so the agent translator can fold it into the agent config hash — in-place rotations (cert-manager updating bytes under the same Secret name) flip the agent config hash and roll the pod.
- The RemoteMCPServer controller watches Secrets (mirrors the existing ModelConfig controller pattern): an in-place CA rotation triggers an immediate RMS reconcile via reverse-lookup, not the 60s `RequeueAfter` tick. `GenerationChangedPredicate` on `.For()` filters out the controller's own Status writes.
- Added `core/pkg/egress` package, gated by the `--mcp-egress-plaintext` flag. When enabled, two entry points resolve a `RemoteMCPServer.spec.url` to the same plaintext URL: `RewriteConfigForAgent` (called in-line by the agent translator before the config Secret is serialized, so the existing config-hash captures the rewrite and rolls the pod on toggle) and `RewriteDialURL` (used by the controller's tool-discovery probe). Both use the RMS's tls-aware effective port so the agent and the controller dial an identical endpoint.
- `addTLSConfiguration` mounts a per-Secret read-only volume at `/etc/ssl/certs/custom/<id>/<key>`, where `<id>` is the Secret name verbatim when it fits the DNS-1123-label grammar and length, or a short SHA-256 prefix otherwise (cert-manager-style names with dots, long names). Previously a single fixed-name volume silently collided when multiple TLS sources referenced different Secrets on the same agent.
- `POST /api/toolservers` accepts an optional `secrets []SecretMaterial` array so operators can create an RMS or MCPServer and its companion Secrets in a single round-trip. Mirrors the existing `ModelConfig` inline-Secret pattern.
- Companion-Secret writes use generic `createOrUpdateCompanionSecrets` + `isOwnedBy` helpers (no longer ModelConfig-specific). OwnerRef matching now requires non-empty UID — safer than the old wildcard match against a stale-UID Secret left over from a delete-recreate.
- All three Create paths (`HandleCreateModelConfig`, `handleCreateRemoteMCPServer`, `handleCreateMCPServer`) close their partial-failure window: a companion-Secret failure rolls back the owner CR via `rollbackOwnerOnCompanionSecretFailure`, so the operator's retry sees a clean slate instead of an `AlreadyExists` on a half-created owner.
- `HandleUpdateModelConfig` similarly closes its partial-failure window with a reorder (companion Secrets written before the Spec Update) plus a sweep pass: after a successful Spec Update, companion Secrets that the prior Spec referenced but the new Spec doesn't get deleted **only if** they carry an OwnerRef back to this ModelConfig. Externally-managed Secrets are left untouched. Cleans up the rename case (`Spec.TLS.CACertSecretRef: ca-v1 → ca-v2`) so old Secrets don't accumulate.

# Out of scope

- mTLS (client certs to upstream).
- ConfigMap-sourced CA bundles.
- UI form fields for `spec.tls` — the TypeScript type binding is in this PR; rendering the form is a UI follow-up.
- Automatic cleanup of companion Secrets created via earlier PUT requests that the UI never surfaces (no GET endpoint exposes existing companion Secrets, so the sweep is intentionally scoped to "Secrets the prior Spec referenced and the new Spec doesn't" — full PUT-replace semantics on `req.Secrets` would silently delete Secrets the UI didn't know about).
author: iplay88keys

title: feat(adk): propagate parent + root context_id headers on outbound A2A calls
body:
## Summary

Stamps two conversation-lineage HTTP headers on every outbound `KAgentRemoteA2ATool` call so a remote peer can correlate the turn with the originating chat conversation across a chain of A2A hops.

- `x-kagent-parent-context-id` — immediate caller's session id (changes per hop)
- `x-kagent-root-context-id` — top-of-chain context_id (stable across hops + across turns of the same conversation)

Closes #1926.

## Why

`KAgentRemoteA2ATool.run_async` ships the outbound A2A message with `context_id = self._last_context_id`, a `uuid4()` minted once per tool instance. The receiving peer has no way to correlate the turn with the chat that started it. Anyone building multi-hop kagent fleets that key per-conversation state (sessions, worker pods, idempotency tokens, cache entries) on a stable identifier today either hand-rolls a `header_provider` on every tool or accepts losing continuity at every hop.

Motivating concrete failure mode: a chat-tier Declarative agent delegates to a router that spawns a per-conversation worker pod (e.g. a `kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox` `SandboxClaim` running `claude --print`); the router has no stable identifier to key the claim on, so a fresh pod spawns every turn and the user-visible symptom is "the agent loses memory between turns" even though the kagent UI thread is the same.

The receive side already copies all inbound HTTP headers into `session.state["headers"]` (set by `A2aAgentExecutor.execute`, lines 541-545), so no server-side plumbing change is needed — a peer that wants the root context_id just reads it from session state.

## What changed

`python/packages/kagent-adk/src/kagent/adk/_remote_a2a_tool.py`

- Two new module-level header constants: `PARENT_CONTEXT_ID_HEADER` and `ROOT_CONTEXT_ID_HEADER`.
- `_build_call_context` now also derives a lineage-headers dict and merges it into the outbound `ClientCallContext` state. The existing `header_provider` callback still wins on overlap, so callers can override lineage when they need to.
- Helper `_build_lineage_headers` derives `parent_context_id` from `tool_context.session.id` and `root_context_id` from the inbound `state["headers"]` (forwarded unchanged when present, falls back to legacy `parent` header for older callers, falls back to caller's own session id when this agent is the chain root). Returns `{}` when no session id is resolvable so the outbound request matches pre-feature behavior on stub tool contexts.

`python/packages/kagent-adk/tests/unittests/test_remote_a2a_tool.py`

- Extends `_MockSession`/`MockToolContext` to expose `id` and `state` so lineage logic is testable.
- Extends `_make_tool` helper to forward an optional `header_provider`.
- New `TestLineageHeaderPropagation` class covers six cases:
  - Root agent stamps own id as parent + root.
  - Mid-chain agent forwards root unchanged + overrides parent with own id.
  - Legacy inbound with only `parent` header promotes it to root.
  - No session id → no lineage headers (non-breaking).
  - `header_provider` overrides lineage when callers need to.
  - End-to-end through `_SubagentInterceptor` onto outbound `http_kwargs.headers`.

## Test plan

- [x] `uv run ruff format --diff` → clean
- [x] `uv run ruff check` → "All checks passed!"
- [x] `uv run pytest packages/kagent-adk/tests/unittests/test_remote_a2a_tool.py -q` → **28 passed**

## Notes for reviewers

- Marked **draft** to gather design feedback first per CONTRIBUTING.md guidance. Happy to iterate on header naming, derivation rules, or scope.
- Backward-compatible: callers that ignore the new headers see no behavior change; callers that opt in just read `session.state["headers"][ROOT_CONTEXT_ID_HEADER]`.
- The export of header-name constants is intentional so BYO consumers (`from kagent.adk._remote_a2a_tool import ROOT_CONTEXT_ID_HEADER`) avoid string-literal drift.
author: Prefix

linked_issues.title: [FEATURE] Propagate parent + root context_id headers on outbound A2A calls
linked_issues.body:
### 📋 Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched the existing issues to avoid creating a duplicate
- [x] By submitting this issue, you agree to follow our Code of Conduct

### 📝 Feature Summary

Propagate two conversation-lineage HTTP headers (`x-kagent-parent-context-id` + `x-kagent-root-context-id`) on outbound `KAgentRemoteA2ATool` calls so a peer agent can correlate a turn with the originating chat conversation across a chain of A2A hops.

### ❓ Problem Statement / Motivation

Today `KAgentRemoteA2ATool.run_async` ships the outbound A2A message with `context_id = self._last_context_id`, which is a `uuid4()` minted once per tool instance at construction (`_remote_a2a_tool.py` line 159). The receiving peer sees that opaque uuid as its own A2A `context_id` and has no way to correlate the turn back to the originating chat conversation.

`_SubagentInterceptor` only forwards two headers today: `x-user-id` and `x-kagent-source: agent` (`_remote_a2a_tool.py` lines 73-84). The receive side already copies all inbound headers into `session.state["headers"]` in `_agent_executor.py` lines 541-545 — so the *plumbing* to consume new headers exists; it is only the outbound-stamping side that lacks lineage data.

Concrete failure mode that motivated this issue: a chat-tier Declarative kagent Agent delegates to a router agent over A2A; the router spawns a per-conversation worker pod (e.g. a kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox `SandboxClaim` running `claude --print`) and wants the same pod to serve every subsequent turn of the same chat thread. Without a stable cross-hop identifier the router has to re-create the worker on every turn — because the tool's self-generated `context_id` is opaque to the chat-tier and changes the moment the chat-tier's tool list is rebuilt. The user-visible symptom is "my coding agent loses memory between turns" even though the kagent UI thread is the same.

title: fix(adk): Propagate access token to subagents and mcp tools
body:
**Overview**
- Fixes an issue where setting the `KAGENT_PROPAGATE_TOKEN` env var for `Agent` CR's using the `python` runtime would only propagate the access token to mcp tools and not to sub agents. 
- Fixes an issue where the setting the `KAGENT_PROPAGATE_TOKEN` env var for the `go` run would not propagate the token to mcp or subagents as it was unimplented.

**Testing**
- Manually tested by deploying a coordinator agent and a subagent that uses the `kagent-tool-server` and making an a2a request to the coordinator agent then inspecting the logs. I added logs [here](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/pull/1858/changes#diff-2c6ac47a132ff8be9df40e1902380810f3d2c8ead904caaf53d22161440903c5R56) and [here](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/pull/1858/changes#diff-aa78af986baa8c0d72522820c58c6a60eb1d656aff48985d4ff3fd683a4312bfR267) for the go runtime and [here](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/pull/1858/changes#diff-e2f83de8fb21d0c7da42da4ab5f1ad96d03fe03abbb40432b80ac2adaf483ba8R90) for the python runtime to validate the allowed header was being added by the interceptor

closes https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/issues/1745
author: jmhbh

linked_issues.title: [FEATURE]  _SubagentInterceptor should forward Authorization header from parent session to sub-agent A2A calls
linked_issues.body:
### 📋 Prerequisites

- [x] I have searched the [existing issues](./issues) to avoid creating a duplicate
- [x] By submitting this issue, you agree to follow our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)

### 📝 Feature Summary

When a supervisor agent dispatches to a sub-agent via `type: Agent`, the `_SubagentInterceptor` should forward the `Authorization` header from the parent agent's `session.state["headers"]`, so that the sub-agent's downstream MCP calls can propagate the caller's JWT end-to-end.

### ❓ Problem Statement / Motivation

When using `allowedHeaders: [Authorization]` on Agent CRs, the user's JWT propagates correctly from the incoming A2A request to outbound MCP calls — but only for **direct** agent invocations. In multi-agent setups where a coordinator (supervisor) dispatches to sub-agents via `type: Agent`, the JWT is silently dropped at the A2A hop.

`_SubagentInterceptor` ([`_remote_a2a_tool.py:63-77`](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/blob/main/python/packages/kagent-adk/src/kagent/adk/_remote_a2a_tool.py#L63-L77)) currently only forwards two headers:
- `x-kagent-source: agent`
- `x-user-id` (from `context.state`)

The `Authorization` header stored in `context.state["headers"]` (populated by `_agent_executor.py` and consumed by `create_header_provider` in `types.py`) is **not forwarded** to the sub-agent.

This means:

| Path | JWT at MCP backend? |
|---|---|
| Browser → agent → MCP | ✅ Yes (`allowedHeaders` works) |
| Browser → coordinator → sub-agent → MCP | ❌ No (JWT lost at coordinator → sub-agent hop) |

### 💡 Proposed Solution

The fix requires two changes — the interceptor alone isn't enough because the headers are never passed into the `ClientCallContext` it receives.

### Change 1: Pass session headers into `ClientCallContext.state`

In `_handle_first_call()` ([line 242](https://github.com/kagent-dev/kagent/blob/main/python/packages/kagent-adk/src/kagent/adk/_remote_a2a_tool.py#L242)) and `_ha

title: fix(bedrock): sanitize tool names to satisfy Bedrock Converse API constraint
body:
Closes #1473.

MCP server tool names often contain dots, colons, or spaces (e.g. `fetch.get_url` or `filesystem:read_file`). Bedrock's Converse API rejects any tool name outside `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+`, so agents wired up to MCP tools hit a hard validation error before the first call goes through.

Added `_sanitize_tool_name()` that replaces invalid characters with underscores and caches the mapping for the duration of a request, same pattern as the existing `_sanitize_tool_id()`. Wired it into `_convert_tools_to_converse()` so every `toolSpec.name` sent to Bedrock is clean. The same map is passed to `_convert_content_to_converse_messages()` so conversation history uses the sanitized names Bedrock expects. A reverse map is built from it so that when Bedrock echoes back a sanitized name in a `toolUse` response, we restore the original name before returning it in the `LlmResponse`, that way the ADK framework can still dispatch to the correctly registered tool.

Tests cover the sanitizer itself (dots, colons, spaces, hyphens, caching, empty input) and the end-to-end flow verifying that the tool spec reaches Bedrock with the sanitized name and the response comes back with the original.
author: mesutoezdil

linked_issues.title: fix(bedrock): sanitize tool names for Bedrock API constraints
linked_issues.body:
## Problem

When using kagent with Amazon Bedrock models, MCP servers (e.g. GitHub Copilot) may produce tool names containing dots, spaces, or other characters that violate Bedrock's API constraints.

Bedrock requires tool names to match `[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+` with a minimum length of 1. Any name outside this pattern causes API errors like:

```
ValidationException: A tool name is invalid. Tool names can only contain letters, numbers, underscores, and hyphens
```

## Root Cause

`KAgentLiteLlm` does not sanitize tool names before passing them to the Bedrock API, and does not sanitize tool names in the conversation history (function call parts) sent in subsequent requests.

## Fix

Override `generate_content_async` in `KAgentLiteLlm` to:
- Sanitize tool names in the outgoing `LlmRequest` history (function call parts in `contents`)
- Sanitize tool names in incoming `LlmResponse` parts

Invalid characters are replaced with `_`. Empty/completely invalid names fall back to `unknown_tool_<idx>`. A warning is logged whenever a name is sanitized, to aid debugging.

## Affected File

`python/packages/kagent-adk/src/kagent/adk/models/_litellm.py`

title: [BUG] Python OTLP exporter hardcodes gRPC, ignores OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL
body:
## Bug Description

The Python agent tracing code in `python/packages/kagent-core/src/kagent/core/tracing/_utils.py` hardcodes the gRPC OTLP exporter via top-level imports:

```python
from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.grpc._log_exporter import OTLPLogExporter
from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.grpc.trace_exporter import OTLPSpanExporter
```

The standard `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_PROTOCOL` and `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL` environment variables are completely ignored. This means agents **cannot export traces over HTTP/protobuf**, which is required by backends like [Langfuse](https://langfuse.com/docs/opentelemetry) that only support OTLP over HTTP (not gRPC).

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Deploy kagent with `otel.tracing.enabled: true` and `otel.tracing.exporter.otlp.protocol: http/protobuf`
2. Point the endpoint to an HTTP-only OTLP backend (e.g., Langfuse `https://cloud.langfuse.com/api/public/otel`)
3. Observe agent pods have `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_PROTOCOL=http/protobuf` set correctly
4. Agent pods still use the gRPC exporter and fail with:

```
opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.grpc.exporter - ERROR - Failed to export traces, error code: StatusCode.UNKNOWN
grpc._channel._InactiveRpcError: <_InactiveRpcError of RPC that terminated with:
    debug_error_string = "UNKNOWN:Error received from peer {grpc_status:2, grpc_message:"Received http2 header with status: 464"}"
```

## Expected Behavior

The `configure()` function should respect `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_PROTOCOL` (or the general `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_PROTOCOL`) and dynamically select the appropriate exporter, per the [OpenTelemetry specification](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/protocol/exporter/):

- `grpc` (default) → `opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.grpc`
- `http/protobuf` → `opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.http`

## Fix

This PR replaces the hardcoded gRPC imports with factory functions (`_create_span_exporter`, `_create_log_exporter`) that resolve the protocol from env vars following the OTel spec precedence: signal-specific > general > default (`grpc`).

The fix applies to both the trace and log exporters. Default behavior (gRPC) is unchanged — this is fully backwards compatible.

## Environment

- kagent version: v0.8.6 (also confirmed on `main` branch)
- Backend: Langfuse Cloud (EU), which only supports OTLP over HTTP/JSON and HTTP/protobuf ([docs](https://langfuse.com/docs/opentelemetry))
author: shmuelarditi

title: Add codespell support with configuration and fixes
body:
Add [codespell](https://github.com/codespell-project/codespell) configuration and fix existing typos.

I personally introduced it to dozens if not hundreds of projects already and so far only positive feedback.

CI workflow has `permissions` set only to `read` so should be safe.

## Changes

### Configuration & Infrastructure
- Added `.codespellrc` with comprehensive skip patterns for caches (`.cache`, `.npm`, `.yarn`), test fixtures (`*/e2e/raw_tables/*`, `*/e2e/snapshot_tables/*`), lock files, SVGs, CSS
- Created GitHub Actions workflow to check spelling on push and PRs
- camelCase/PascalCase ignore-regex to avoid flagging Go/TypeScript identifiers (`HasTable`, `OrderIn`, `repoRes`, etc.)

### Domain-Specific Whitelist
Added legitimate terms that codespell flags as typos:
- `convertor`/`convertors` - project's deliberate spelling convention for Go types and filenames (`DataConvertor`, `*_convertor.go`)
- `crypted` - variable name in AES encrypt/decrypt functions
- `te` - Tapd API field name
- `thur` - Thursday abbreviation in Grafana dashboard SQL

### Typo Fixes

**Ambiguous typos fixed manually** (11 distinct typos across 20 files):
- `convered` -> `converted` (jenkins build model comments)
- `covert` -> `converted` (issue_status_history log message)
- `Colume` -> `Column` (migrationhelper error messages)
- `poping` -> `popping` (api_client comment)
- `limt` -> `limit` (6 connection model struct tag comments)
- `faile` -> `failed` (pipeline error messages)
- `ths` -> `the` (service.go comment)
- `reloades` -> `reloads` (blueprint.go comment)
- `verifi` -> `verify` (job_test.go comment)
- `Troughput` -> `Throughput` (Grafana dashboard display text)
- `actived`/`activedColor` -> `activated`/`activatedColor` (onboard styled components)

**Non-ambiguous typos fixed via `codespell -w`** (67 fixes across 47 files):
Common fixes include: `doens't` -> `doesn't`, `faild` -> `failed`, `Internel` -> `Internal`, `mintue` -> `minute`, `mulitple`/`mutiple`/`muliple` -> `multiple`, `pipline`/`pipleline`/`pipepline` -> `pipeline`, `envioronment` -> `environment`, `Reveiw` -> `Review`, `serverity` -> `severity`

### Historical Context
This project has had 119 prior commits fixing typos manually, demonstrating the value of automated spell-checking.

## Testing

Codespell passes with zero errors after all fixes.

---

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) and love to typos free code
author: yarikoptic

title: feat(azuredevops): treat partially succeeded builds as successful pipeline runs

> Please complete _ALL_ items in this checklist, and remove before submitting

- [x] I have read through the [Contributing Documentation](https://devlake.apache.org/community/).

### Summary
What does this PR do?
It will consider partially succeeded builds as successful CI/CD results for Azure Devops.
Changes are made for python and go.

### Does this close any open issues?
Closes https://github.com/apache/incubator-devlake/issues/8575
author: parth9033

linked_issues.title: [Feature][Azure DevOps] Deployments after partially succeeded builds
linked_issues.body:
### Search before asking

- [x] I had searched in the [issues](https://github.com/apache/incubator-devlake/issues?q=is%3Aissue) and found no similar feature requirement.


### Use case

In my azure DevOps pipelines we have optional jobs that are allowed to fail, but to not block the deployment.

### Description

With https://github.com/apache/incubator-devlake/pull/6548/files#diff-890d51a93f4a7de6904d29ebaba0b715a13151a9fa01456de7a07797d3f0d2a2 any `PartiallySucceeded` build is defined as `FAILURE`. 

If a build that actually does a production deployment is not detected as such, then the DORA metrics are not correct. 

Ideally it would be part of the scope definition, how to tread `PartiallySucceeded` builds.

### Related issues

_No response_
